data.title: Great Hall, Faneuil Hall Closures: April 2026
data.description: Due to scheduled events, the Great Hall will be closed on April 9, 10, 12, 13, 18, and 23. The Visitor Center Desk on the market floor will remain open.

data.title: Keonehe‘ehe‘e (Sliding Sands) trail is closed
data.description: Due to the recent storms, Keonehe‘ehe‘e (Sliding Sands) trail is closed until further notice. You may access the crater floor via the Halemau‘u Trail. All other trails are open.

data.title: Paradise Road Status: Gate closed at Longmire, check tomorrow for status.
data.description: 04/01/2026 The road is open to Longmire. All vehicles, including 4WD and AWD, must carry tire chains and be ready to use them. The gate at Longmire to Paradise is closed for the night to support public safety. Check in the morning for updated status of the gate at Longmire, Thursday 04/02/2026

data.title: 49 Palms Trail Open
data.description: As of April 1st, the 49 Palms Oasis trail has fully reopened.

data.title: Haleakalā Visitor Center Closed 2/19 through the week of 5/17
data.description: Haleakalā Visitor Center (elevation 9,740 ft) will be temporarily closed for a building improvement project from February 19th through the week of May 17th. Sunrise operations will continue as normal and restrooms will remain open. Headquarters Visitor Center (elevation 7,000 ft) is open.

data.title: Headquarters Visitor Center is open
data.description: Headquarters Visitor Center is open following the recent storms.

data.title: Stage 3 Water Use Restrictions Begin April 1, 2026
data.description: Effective April 1, the South Rim has transitioned to Stage 3 Water Restrictions. Camper Services at Mather Campground has closed; water spigots at Mather Campground are off; restrooms at Mather Campground remain open; Park concessioners, partners, visitors, and residents are asked to continue water-reduction protocols.

data.title: Dogs Prohibited on Grand Parade Trails from April 1 to September 15
data.description: The Grand Parade Trails are closed to dogs from April 1 to September 15 to protect breeding birds nesting in the meadows. Nesting songbirds see dogs as predators, which can stress the birds and lead to less productive or even failed nests.

data.title: Seasonal road closures
data.description: The following roads are closed for the season: Roaring Fork Motor Nature Trail, Little Greenbrier Road, Heintooga Ridge Road, Parson Branch Road, and Rich Mountain Road.

data.title: Change in Hours for Summer Season
data.description: Due to the potential of excessive summer heat, starting Monday May 4, The Lower Cliff Dwellings trail will close at 12:00P.M. The visitor center & park will remain open until 4:00P.M. These hours will be in place until Saturday October 3.

data.title: Lane closures on Piney Branch Parkway
data.description: In late March and early April, there will be temporary closures on Piney Branch Parkway for project work with DC Water and the Federal Highway Administration. As much as possible, these temporary closures will not occur during rush hour times.

data.title: Cunard Road Closure
data.description: Cunard Road is currently closed to vehicle traffic, and will reopen on April 6th. Expect delays for the first 2 weeks as one section will be one lane with a flagger, but after that both lanes will be open.

data.title: More Areas Reopen in Hawaiʻi Volcanoes National Park
data.description: For more details on current closures, click View All Alerts. Some sections of the park have reopened at the summit of Kīlauea, Mauna Loa Road and Chain of Craters Road. Work continues to clear and repair other areas impacted by storm damage and the recent eruption.

data.title: Prescribed Burn
data.description: The Sierra Vista Ranger District will resume operations for the Border II Prescribed Burn project on April 2, 2026, if weather conditions are favorable. Light, persistent smoke is expected to be present with some periods of heavier smoke, which may be seen coming over the west side of the Huachuca Mountains. No closures are expected.

data.title: Cave Tour Reservations
data.description: Advance cave tour reservations up until May 31st are available. June reservations will become available on May 1st. Reservations are strongly recommended. Reservations full? Choose a day to come to the park early in the day to purchase a same day cave tour ticket.

data.title: Catoctin Mountain Park announces temporary closures April 2 - 6, 2026
data.description: Portions of Catoctin Mountain Park will be closed at approximately noon on Thursday, April 2. Closures are scheduled to continue through approximately noon on Monday, April 6, 2026.

data.title: High Fire Danger and Fire Ban
data.description: Due to high fire danger throughout the park, a fire ban has been put into place. As a result, the lighting of campfires is not allowed at this time. Charcoal grills are permissible as long as the charcoal is attended during use.

data.title: Temporary closures for wildfires
data.description: Fire crews are actively working to suppress the Rabbit Creek Fire and Fox Gap Fire. Temporary closures for public safety related to fires include: Backcountry Campsite 16; Rabbit Creek Trail from Abrams Creek Campground to Hannah Mtn. Trail; Cooper Road Trail from Abrams Creek Campground to Little Bottoms Trail; Abrams Creek Campground Road

data.title: Aid Box Rock Climbing Area Closed
data.description: The Aid Box rock climbing area is temporarily closed due to a recent rock topple.

data.title: Brock Road Lane Closure to begin April 13
data.description: Lane Closure on the Spotsylvania Battlefield beginning April 20, 2026. We are expecting that beginning April 13, 2026, there will be a lane closure along Brock Road between the Grant Drive/Hancock Road and Old Block House Road intersections for utility line installation. This closure will include the Laurel Hill Trail on the Spotsylvania Battlefield, and is expected to last for approximately two weeks.

data.title: Closures on Park Central Road
data.description: Catoctin Mountain Park will begin repaving work on Park Central Road on Monday, March 30. The work will include closures of sections of Park Central Road between the Visitor Center and the Hog Rock parking lot. The closures will be in place Monday through Friday. On weekends, the road will be fully open. Work is expected to continue for 4-6 weeks. The Visitor Center will remain open throughout the project.

data.title: Playground in Pine Grove Picnic Area Closed
data.description: The park is improving accessibility at the playground in Pine Grove Picnic Area. During construction, until approximately the end of April 2026, the playground is closed and construction vehicles may be in the area. Use caution when hiking in the area and follow all posted closure notices.

data.title: Theodore Roosevelt National Park moves to cashless fee collection
data.description: Starting May 1, Theodore Roosevelt National Park will accept only credit cards, debit cards, or digital payments for entrance fees, campground fees, and Interagency Passes. Cash and check payments will no longer be accepted.

data.title: Thaddeus Kosciuszko National Memorial Closed for the Season
data.description: Closed for the season. The site will reopen Saturday, April 4, 2026. Usual site hours: Saturday & Sunday, 12–4 pm.

data.title: Prescribed Burn in Big Meadows
data.description: Weather permitting, fire personnel plan to conduct a prescribed fire Wednesday, April 1, in Big Meadows (mile 51). Visitors should slow their speeds and turn their headlights on when driving through smoke. Access to Rapidan Road and areas of Big Meadows west of Rapidan Road will be restricted during the prescribed fire. Areas of Big Meadows west of Rapidan Road will be closed during the operation through Thursday, April 2.

data.title: Red Hills Visitor Center (TMD) theater closed for repairs
data.description: The theater curtains and supporting mechanisms in the Red Hills Visitor Center (west district of the park) have broken and fallen down. The theater will be closed until repairs have been made and the area is safe again for visitors. Theater talks have been postponed for the time being, outdoor events and programs will continue as scheduled.

data.title: Road to Rim Village is Temporarily CLOSED Due to Incoming Snowstorm
data.description: The road from Park Headquarters to Rim Village is temporarily CLOSED due to a major incoming storm. No view of the lake is accessible by vehicle at this time. According to the National Weather Service, travel is not advised. If you must drive in the park, please use extreme caution and be on the lookout for snowplows. All Oregon Chain Laws apply.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Road from HQ to Rim Village Remains CLOSED.

data.title: Routine Winter Service Levels in Effect
data.description: Roads and areas of the park may be closed or inaccessible during the winter, Please check the park's current conditions page to learn more.

data.title: Huffman Prairie Flying Field Intersection to Natural Prairie Closed 3/29/26
data.description: Wright-Patterson Air Force Base plans to close the intersection of Marl and Pylon Road intermittently over the next ten days. This closing will not impact access to Huffman Prairie Flying Field parking, restrooms and walking paths. During periods of closure, please use the main parking lot, as smaller lots along Pylon Road may be unavailable.

data.title: Closure: Davison Road
data.description: As of March 30, Davison Road is closed the intersection with Trillium Falls until further notice due to a landslide south of the Gold Bluffs Beach campground. Reopening of road is unknown at this time. Vehicle access to Fern Canyon is completely blocked. Hiking to Fern Canyon via the 12-mile loop on James Irvine Trail remains open but be advised emergency response will be extended.

data.title: Great Basin Visitor Center Closed April 1st
data.description: Due to a scheduled power outage in the area, the Great Basin Visitor Center will be closed Wed. April 1st, 2026. If power is restored before 12:45pm, the visitor center will reopen at that time.

data.title: Administrative Closure of Areas due to Nesting Shorebird Season
data.description: Effective 4/2/26: The closure includes the following areas within the boundary of the National Seashore : * North End * All island interior beach habitat along the northern section of Assateague Island National Seashore (north of Assateague State Park) is closed to all entry between KM 0.5 - KM 9.5. * The island interior beach habitats between KM 21.8 - 22.8 and 23.5 - 24.5 of the MD OSV Zone are closed to all entry. This includes habitat from the existing OSV boundary west to the vegetated shrub line.

data.title: Annual/Seasonal Canyon Closures
data.description: The following are closed to entry through 8/31/2026 to protect sensitive resources: the heads of Fivemile Wash & Burro Wash descending east 2 miles; Shinob Canyon + all routes descending into Shinob (including Na-gah, Nighthawk, & Timpie); and Arch Nemesis Canyon, including the route exiting at Capitol Gorge + all direct tributary canyons.

data.title: Presidio Trust Trails Annual Coyote Pupping Season is April to October
data.description: To prevent dog-coyote conflicts, avoid seasonally closed sections of the Park Trail and the Bay Area Ridge Trail to dog walking. Explore open dog-friendly trails instead and remember to keep dogs on leash. Visit the Presidio Trust's website for more details.

data.title: Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive closed for annual winter/spring season
data.description: The Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive is closed to vehicles for the annual winter and spring closure. The drive is open for hiking and walk-in recreation during the vehicle closure.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive annual winter/spring road closure

data.title: Hurricane Ridge Road Status
data.description: Hurricane Ridge Road is closed due to incoming storm. During the period of March 30 through April 30, Hurricane Ridge Road may open or close depending on road conditions, weather, and staffing. Check back here or call 360-565-3131 for updates.

data.title: Playalinda District will be closed due to Artemis Launch
data.description: Titusville, FL — On Tuesday, March 31, the Playalinda Beach District of Canaveral National Seashore will be closed at the request of NASA Launch Security in support of the Artemis II launch. The Playalinda Beach District will reopen to the public following a successful launch. During this closure, no backcountry hiking permits will be issued from either district of the park. Canaveral National Seashore is committed to supporting NASA’s security measures throughout launch operations.

data.title: Pile Burning at Mitchell Hill and Hazard Fuels Work at American Camp
data.description: Burn operations at Mitchell Hill are expected to begin on April 6 and conclude April 10, weather permitting. Smoke may be visible from nearby roads and trails during operations. Trail closures will be in effect. At American Camp, crews will use heavy machinery, chainsaws, and other mechanical equipment, which may generate loud noise. A section of the trail leading to Eagle Cove will be temporarily closed during operations.

data.title: Fort Spokane Museum and Visitor Center to Close for Repairs
data.description: The Fort Spokane Museum and Visitor Center in Lake Roosevelt National Recreation Area is temporarily closed for critical historic preservation repairs. Dating to 1880, the landmark building originally served as the fort's guardhouse and now operates as the primary hub to introduce visitors to the park and its many resources.

data.title: SUP application fee increases to $150 beginning May 1, 2026
data.description: Steamtown National Historic Site will adjust its Special Use Permit (SUP) application fee from $50 to $150 effective May 1, 2026. This change reflects the administrative cost of processing and issuing permits and aligns with National Park Service cost recovery policy. This is not a new or expanded public-use fee. For questions, contact STEA_permits@nps.gov.

data.title: Island Ford Trail Closures Winter 2025-2026
data.description: All trails at the Island Ford Unit of CRNRA will be closed December 8 - 11. Crews will be felling hazardous trees; for visitor safety and project efficiency please stay off the trails. The Visitor Center remains open 9am - 5pm. Trails will be open until January 5. From January 5 through Spring 2026, Island Ford trails will be closed as we improve the trail system and add 2.2 miles of new trails. For more information, visit chattahoocheeparks.org/iftrails.

data.title: Lincoln Home Phone Line
data.description: The Lincoln Home phone number is experiencing technical difficulties. If you would like to call the site, please call 217-391-3221 and leave a voicemail.

data.title: Beach fires temporarily banned due to dry conditions and statewide burn ban
data.description: Cape Hatteras National Seashore has temporarily banned beach fires due to abnormally dry conditions that may increase the risk of beach fires spreading into dune vegetation, forested areas, or developed neighborhoods adjacent to Seashore beaches. The N.C. Forest Service has also issued a ban on all open fires across the state.

data.title: Park Phone Temporarily Down
data.description: Park phone lines are temporarily down. Until connection is restored, visitors can call 201-458-3475 for general information Wednesday - Sunday, 9:00 AM - 4:30 PM EST.

data.title: Road Construction Near the Park
data.description: There is road construction near the park visitor center along 111th St. and S. Cottage Avenue. Please use caution and allow extra travel time when visiting. Construction is expected to last through the fall.

data.title: Blue Basin Overlook Trail Closed
data.description: The 3.25 - mile Blue Basin Overlook trail in the Sheep Rock unit is closed until further notice. A mudslide and trail washouts have created unsafe conditions for hiking. The shorter 1.3- mile Island In Time trail remains open.

data.title: Loop Road OPEN; Caves Area Closed
data.description: The majority of the 7-mile Loop Road is OPEN to vehicles, with the exception of the short spur road to the Caves. Caves remain closed until June due to hazardous ice and snow. The Loop Road is also open to walking, hiking, wheelchairs. Please plan accordingly.

data.title: Conditions and Construction Affecting Cataract Canyon Takeouts
data.description: The primitive, dirt take-out at North Wash is undergoing construction this spring which might cause delays of up to 5 hours. Rafters are advised to use the foot trail past Highway 95 for passenger & gear drop-off (steep incline), continue on to Bullfrog, or plan their take-out when construction is not active: (Friday–Sunday or before 7am / after 6pm Monday–Thursday).

data.title: Ongoing Road Projects and Helene Recovery
data.description: Parkway areas closed for ongoing road maintenance, construction, and Helene recovery projects.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Ongoing Road Projects and Helene Recovery

data.title: Park Pass Not Needed
data.description: As Congaree does not have an entrance fee, we do not have staff dedicated to selling or issuing park passes. We cannot guarantee that staff will be available to sell or issue a pass at the time of your visit. Annual and lifetime passes may be obtained at any national park that charges an entrance fee. Passes may also be purchased on Recreation.gov (digital and physical).

data.title: Conditions and Construction Affecting Cataract Canyon Takeouts
data.description: Hite Ramp is closed. The primitive, dirt take-out at North Wash is undergoing construction this spring which might cause delays of up to 5 hours. Boaters are advised to use the foot trail past Highway 95 for passenger & gear drop-off (steep incline), continue on to Bullfrog, or plan their take-out when construction is not active: (Friday–Sunday or before 7am / after 6pm Monday–Thursday).

data.title: Construction Delays
data.description: There are heavy traffic delays in both directions before entering the park., Please use caution.

data.title: Temporary Emergency Fire Restrictions
data.description: Due to dry conditions and high fire danger, open fires are prohibited in Wind Cave National Park until further notice. Gas stoves used in or on fire resistant containers are permitted only in the Elk Mountain Campground and the Picnic Area. No smoking is allowed except in vehicles or on paved or concrete areas next to the visitor center. Fires of any kind on trails or in backcountry areas are prohibited. This includes smoking, gas stoves and open fires.

data.title: Long-term Closures Due to Wildfires
data.description: The following areas are closed until further notice: [1] South Rim Campground. [2] East Portal Campground. [3] Uplands Trail from South Rim Campground to road crossing south of Tomichi point. [4] Rim Rock Trail from South Rim Campground to Tomichi Point.

data.title: Off Season Tours by Appointment Unavailable on Dates from March - May
data.description: Off season tours by appointment are not available on the following dates in March, April, and May: March 9 - April 3; April 6 - 10; April 14 - 16; May 15 - 27.

data.title: Lassen National Park Highway under Seasonal Closure
data.description: The main park road (Hwy 89) is closed to through travel due to winter snow accumulation. Access to the Kohm Yah-mah-nee Visitor Center from the southwest entrance and Devastated Area from the northwest entrance at Manzanita Lake is open. Active spring road clearing operations are taking place. The road will not entirely reopen until late spring or early summer.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Lassen National Park Highway Closed for Winter

data.title: Burn Ban in Effect
data.description: BURN BAN in Gulpha Gorge Campground within Hot Springs National Park, under the jurisdiction of the National Park Service. This temporary public use closure is in response to drought conditions within the area. No campfires of any kind are allowed. Gas and/or charcoal grills are currently allowed. This closure will take effect March 26, 2026, at 12:00pm and will remain in effect until further notice.

data.title: Beach Drive open for Driving Day on April 11, 2026
data.description: On April 11, from 11 am to 4 pm, the entire length of Beach Drive in Rock Creek Park will be open for a scenic driving day for visitors who prefer to enjoy the park from their cars. Those who frequently bike or hike in these areas should plan accordingly.

data.title: Alava Trail Closure
data.description: The Mount Alava trail and Fatifati trail from the Fagasa pass to the summit is closed for trail improvement work until further notice. Visitors can hike to the Alava trail from the Old Vatia trail, it is a much steeper and more rugged climb. Other suggested hiking alternatives include Upper and Lower Sauma trails and the Tuafanua trail.

data.title: Launch Ramp Updates
data.description: Lake level changes will influence the functionality of all launch ramps. Find updated status of all launch ramps at the link.

data.title: Phone System Issues and Updated Contact Number
data.description: Dayton Aviation Heritage is currently experiencing phone system issues. 937-225-7705 is not in service, and so a temporary number is in place. Please call 937-345-0433 for information on the park.

data.title: Temporary Closure at Mni Owe Sni
data.description: The parking area at Mni Owe Sni (Coldwater Spring Unit) is temporarily closed to motor vehicles as park staff assess the site for protection of natural and cultural resources. The area will remain open to pedestrian use during this time.

data.title: Winter Closures
data.description: The following areas are closed for the winter season: [1] South Rim Road beyond the visitor center. [2] All North Rim facilities and vehicle access. North Rim Road is closed to vehicles and gated at the park boundary. Pedestrians and bicycles are currently allowed on closed roads, conditions permitting. Use caution.

data.title: Beat the Heat!
data.description: Make sure to take weather into consideration when visiting. Carry water and use sunscreen to reduce the risk of heat injuries during your visit.

data.title: Photo Season
data.description: Just a reminder as the weather is starting to get nice, and people are thinking about where to take graduation, wedding, prom or formal, pregnancy, birthday, and any other type of photography or filming! We do have rules and regulations on what is or is not allowed and whether or not you need a permit. Violations may be ticketed. Please check our website under Plan Your Visit, Permits and Reservations!!! https://www.nps.gov/frhi/planyourvisit/permits.htm

data.title: Dead Horse Bay (Brooklyn) Remains Closed to the Public
data.description: The entire southern area of Dead Horse Bay, including Glass Bottle Beach, is closed to the public. Dead Horse Bay is located in Brooklyn, NYC.

data.title: Mount Baldy Closure
data.description: Closure: The Mount Baldy Access Site will be closed from late March through mid-June, 2026, for sand removal and shoreline stabilization work.

data.title: Winter Hours
data.description: Fort Stanwix Visitor Center/Fort are now closed for the winter season but park grounds will remain open and accessible. The Visitor Center reopens on Wednesday, April 1. The Fort reopens on Tuesday, April 14. Please check the park website for reopening updates.

data.title: Nightly closure of 23rd Street from Independence Ave. to Lincoln Memorial
data.description: 23rd Street SW will be closed to all vehicles between Independence Avenue and the Lincoln Memorial nightly from 8:30 p.m. to 5 a.m. for emergency Pepco gas line repairs. The work will begin on Tuesday, March 24 and is expected to take approximately four weeks. For additional information, please contact Pepco at 202-872-2680.
