data.title: Gallatin House Hours Changing
data.description: The Gallatin House and Visitor Center will only be open Saturdays and Sundays until further notice, 9 AM to 5 PM. The grounds and trails will be available from sunrise to sunset year-round.

data.title: Stage 1 Burn Restrictions
data.description: Discharge of fireworks are prohibited. Only campfires at campsites within a fire ring are permitted. Charcoal fires within enclosed grills are permitted. Portable stoves are permitted.

data.title: Winter Road Closures
data.description: The Wheeler Peak Scenic Drive is open to Osceola Ditch Trailhead. Snake Creek Road is closed for the season at the park boundary. Click "more" for current road and campground closures.

data.title: Barataria Visitor Center and Trails are Closed
data.description: Because of upcoming demolition and construction of the trails and Visitor Center, all trails at the Barataria Preserve and the Visitor Center are closed, with the exception of Twin Canals Trail. Please join us Wednesdays and Thursdays 10:00 am - 2:00 pm for programming and guided hikes along the Wetland Trace trail at 1:00 pm at the temporary Town Hall (at the Lafitte Barataria Museum location) and Wetland Trace, 4917 City Park Drive, Jean Lafitte, 70067.

data.title: Partial closure of the Rose Garden from March 31 until early summer
data.description: The planting beds, lawns, and paths of the Rose Garden will be under construction beginning in late March 2026. This rehabilitation will restore the crisp, level character of the garden, improve access and infrastructure, and re-install historic rose and peony varieties. During this period, the Burial Site viewing area should remain available, but occasional disruptions may restrict public access. The graves, marble monument, pet graves, and sundial will not be impacted by this project.

data.title: Temporary Museum Closure
data.description: The park museum in the visitor center is temporarily closed for the installation of new exhibits. All other areas within the visitor center such as the welcome desk, theater, and bookstore will remain open during the project. The museum will reopen in the Spring of 2026.

data.title: Trail update 3/23/26
data.description: Most trails are open and ready for the public. Those that remain closed are the Charleston Road/ Goudey Trail past the Cherokee Path and the portion of Cherokee Path between the stockade fort and the Charleston Road.

data.title: Winter Seasonal Road Closures
data.description: The following roads are closed for the winter season or due to current snow conditions: Mineral King Rd, Highway 180 between Grant Grove and Cedar Grove, Crescent Meadow and Moro Rock Roads, and Panoramic Point Road.

data.title: NPS 300 Road Conditions
data.description: NPS 300 to Big Tubes is dry and passable for most vehicles.

data.title: George Washington Inaugural Bible
data.description: The George Washington Inaugural Bible is currently offsite.

data.title: Sidewalk scaffolding at Federal Hall
data.description: Protective sidewalk scaffolding has been erected around the building to protect the public from falling materials.

data.title: Area and Trail Closures
data.description: Due to wind storm damage, sections of the Lenape Trail, Raccoon Trail, Buzzards Trail, and Horse-Shoe Trail are closed. The ESA parking area and the Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) Pavilion are closed.

data.title: Little Yellow Creek Bridge Closure - Construction Underway
data.description: Work to replace the Little Yellow Creek Bridge on Bartlett Park Road has begun. Bartlett Park remains open and accessible, but visitors will see and hear construction activities. The road and areas south of the bridge, including Marsee Cemetery, will not be accessible during construction.

data.title: Oregon Caves opens for the 2026 season
data.description: Oregon Caves will open March 21, 2026. Cave tours will be available Thursdays thru Mondays from 10:00 am to 4:00 pm and closed on Tuesdays and Wednesdays. Feel free to call 541-592-5125 for more information.

data.title: Malaquite Campground and dump station to be closed for road work in mid-May
data.description: The Malaquite Campground and dump station will be temporarily closed from approximately May 6-16, 2026, for road resurfacing/paving project being conducted by the Federal Highway Administration.

data.title: Cuyahoga River Downstream from Lock 29
data.description: There is a dangerous strainer in the main channel of the rapids downstream of Lock 29. It is parallel to the flow of the river and can be difficult to see from upriver. Scout the river before you launch by walking north on the Towpath Trail. Remember to check river conditions before you go. River water temperatures are still in the 40s.

data.title: Oasis of Mara Trail Partial Closure
data.description: Due to a heavy flooding event, the Oasis of Mara Trail is closed past the Oasis.

data.title: Recommendations for Spring Break Season
data.description: Spring break season (mid-March - mid-April) brings exceptionally high visitation rates. Visitors who plan ahead and pack their patience are in the best position to have a great park experience. Arrive before 10:00 am or after 3:00 pm to avoid long wait times and full parking lots. The shuttle service begins on March 19; when the main visitor parking lots are full, visitors will be required to utilize the shuttle. If all parking lots fill, cars will then be turned around until more spaces become available.

data.title: Shuttle Service Begins March 19
data.description: Bandelier National Monument's free shuttle service to Frijoles Canyon begins on Thursday, March 19, 2026. The shuttle boards at the Frey Trail Parking Lot starting at 10:30 am. The shuttle is not mandatory unless the parking lots in the canyon are filled up. If all parking lots fill both in Frijoles Canyon and the Frey Trail, cars will then be turned around at the park entrance until more spaces become available. We appreciate your patience while navigating an influx in visitation.

data.title: Temporary Closure of Petroglyph Boardwalk
data.description: We will be working to replace the petroglyph boardwalk beginning on October 27th, 2025, through June 1st, 2026. During this time, the boardwalk will be closed for renovations.

data.title: Tioga Road (Hwy 120 through the park) and Glacier Point Road are closed
data.description: Tioga Road (continuation of Highway 120 through the park), Glacier Point Road, and Mariposa Grove Road are closed for the season due to snow. Snow is possible on other roads; be prepared for tire chain requirements and other road closures. Call 209/372-0200 (then 1, 1) to check road conditions.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Tioga and Glacier Point Roads winter closure

data.title: Storm Damage
data.description: Some of our trails are open but most of our eastside trails are inaccessible. Please use caution throughout the site especially when hiking, broken tree parts continue to fall.

data.title: Traffic Delays - Gardner River High Bridge
data.description: Expect a single lane of traffic with up to 15-minute delays, 24 hours a day, seven days a week, from April 1 through late October 2026. Oversized/overweight vehicles (8'6"+ wide, 75'+ long, 80,000 lbs+ gross vehicle weight) will not be able to cross the bridge during this time.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Traffic Delays - Gardner River High Bridge

data.title: Park Ski Trails Ungroomed
data.description: All ski trails in the Grand Marais and Munising areas are currently ungroomed due to deep snow drifts and tree debris.

data.title: Temporary Closure of Right Lane of Clara Barton Parkway
data.description: Temporary Closure of the Clara Barton Parkway Right Lane at Site 17, wooded areas between C&O Canal and Lock 10 parking lot, north to Lock 14, required to address ongoing safety concerns from DC Water emergency sanitary sewer overflow & bypass and pipe repair operations. Click on "More" below for specific details on the closure.

data.title: Blasting in Memorial
data.description: Blasting activity is occurring in the Coronado National Forest, Huachuca Mountains, and within Coronado National Memorial. Visitors may hear loud noise and notice dust in the air.

data.title: Spring 2026 Prescribed Fire Notice at Lake Roosevelt
data.description: Lake Roosevelt National Recreation Area may implement the following prescribed burn during the spring of 2026.

data.title: Carriage Roads closed to all use
data.description: Starting March 19, the National Park Service will close carriage roads to all users until further notice to protect the roads during spring thaw, also known as “mud season.”

data.title: Trip-planning tips for 2026
data.description: Planning to visit Arches this year? To ensure the best experience, please anticipate potentially long wait times at the entrance station. If trailhead parking is full, plan to return a little later. Please avoid parking on vegetation and roadways to help preserve this beautiful place for everyone to enjoy.

data.title: State Route 20 Closed MP130
data.description: State Route 20 is closed at milepost 130 (Colonial Creek Campground) in both lanes. For more information click the link to the WSDOT real time map.

data.title: Operating Hours
data.description: The seashore is currently operating on the hours of 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM. Last entry into the seashore is at 7:00 pm. On March 21, operating hours for Playalinda Beach will be 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Playalinda District will follow this schedule until the successful launch of Artemis II.

data.title: Scam Warning - Book ferry tickets through concessioner site only
data.description: Valid ferry tickets can only be purchased through the website www.cumberlandislandferry.com. Check site carefully before purchasing. Only valid tickets will be honored.

data.title: Passenger Ferry Dock Closed
data.description: The ferry dock servicing the Cape Lookout Lighthouse area is closed for repairs. Visitors will have to use an alternative route, which includes walking about 100 feet on soft sand to access the boardwalk. Visitors should wear sturdy, closed toe shoes because of potential hazards.

data.title: Trip-planning tips for 2026
data.description: Planning to visit Canyonlands this year? To ensure the best experience, please anticipate potentially long wait times at the entrance station. If overlook or trailhead parking is full, plan to return a little later. Please avoid parking on vegetation and roadways to help preserve this beautiful place for everyone to enjoy.

data.title: Waterside Theatre Temporarily Closed
data.description: The Waterside Theatre is temporarily closed, due to a roofing project. Once the project is completed, the area will reopen.

data.title: Fort Pickens Campground Restroom A Loop Closure
data.description: The park is remodeling the interior of the restroom structure at loop A. There will portable restroom and shower trailers set up in this area. All other restrooms will remain open for campers with reservations during this time.

data.title: Trail Restrooms Closed
data.description: Restrooms located at the North intersection of Old Carriage Road Trail and Matildaville Trail are closed due a water leak. There are portable toilets available at this location. Restrooms located at the Visitor Center are open between 8:30a.m - 5:30 p.m.

data.title: China Beach Construction Phase 2 Begins in July 2025
data.description: Project continues with improvements to the lower terrace and picnic areas, rehabbing the interior and exterior of the bathhouse, and installing seating and foot wash station near beach. Projects are expected to take 16-18 months. See link for details.

data.title: Most Park Roads Closed to All Vehicles
data.description: Spring plowing has begun and most roads are closed to all vehicles until mid-April. The road between the North and Northeast entrances remains open and is open year-round to regular vehicles.

data.title: Cadillac Summit Road Closed for Construction
data.description: The Cadillac Summit Road is closed by construction for all uses, including pedestrians and cyclists, from the Park Loop Road to just prior to a parking lot at the summit. The road is already closed to personal vehicles for the off season.

data.title: Phone Service Unavailable
data.description: Phone service at Puʻukoholā Heiau National Historic Site is currently unavailable. If you need to contact the park, please email us at puhe_info@nps.gov and a staff member will respond as soon as possible. We appreciate your patience while service is being restored.

data.title: Kirby Storter Closure
data.description: Starting March 23rd, the Kirby Storter boardwalk will be closed for debris removal. This closure will include what remains of the boardwalk and the north side of the parking lot. The south side of the parking lot, including the comfort station and picnic tables will remain open to the public. The closure for this cleanup is expected to last anywhere from 2 to 4 weeks. Thank you for your understanding and patience.

data.title: Ft Washington Pk will temporarily close on select days from 4 p.m.- 6 a.m.
data.description: The National Park Service will temporarily close Fort Washington Park between 4:00 pm and 6:00 am on select days from March 16, 2026, to April 15, 2026, for deer management operations. These closures protect visitors and staff and follow the White-tailed Deer Management Plan. NPS staff will enforce closures with signage and inspections. Public cooperation is appreciated.

data.title: Part of Piscataway Park to close 4PM-6AM on select days beginning Mar 13
data.description: The National Park Service will temporarily close parts of Piscataway Park, including the Marshall Hall Boat Ramp, from 4:00 pm–6:00 am on select days between March 16, 2026, and April 15, 2026 for deer management. This ensures visitor and staff safety per the White-tailed Deer Management Plan. NPS staff and signs will enforce closures.

data.title: OSV Status
data.description: 3/15/26 MD OSV Open to State Line, VA South OSV partially open. Closure begins at the old Coast Guard Station and extends to the hook. North OSV closed.

data.title: Telephone System is Experiencing Connectivity Issues
data.description: The phone system at Sagamore Hill is experiencing connectivity issues. Please contact the park via email at sahi_information@nps.gov when phones are out of service.

data.title: Restrooms and Water Not Available at Visitor Center
data.description: Due to construction, the Visitor Center Restrooms and water fountains are currently unavailable. Temporary restroom facilities (not wheelchair accessible*) are located to the South East of the visitor center, by the flagpole near the parking lot. Please bring plenty of water for yourself and your group. *Wheelchair accessible restrooms are located at the Lava Flow Campground, as well as the Devils Orchard & Tree Molds parking lots.

data.title: Bumpass Hell Trail Closed
data.description: The Bumpass Hell Trail is under a seasonal closure due to accumulated winter snow. This is an annual closure to prevent injury from treacherous trail conditions. The trail typically remains closed through mid-July. Learn more

data.title: Roads to Juniper Lake and Warner Valley Closed for Repairs
data.description: Roads to Juniper Lake and Warner Valley areas are closed to vehicle access due to needed repairs. Roads leading to Juniper Lake and Warner Valley sustained significant damage outside of the park with no ETA for repair.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Roads to Butte Lake, Juniper Lake, and Warner Valley Closed for Winter

data.title: Road Construction Begins
data.description: In late March, simultaneous road improvements begin along the northern end of the Park Road, and the access roads to Cedar Tree Tower and Far View sites in the south. Travel delays up to 30 minutes are possible. Click the link for the park map.

data.title: Pet Restrictions, Beach Driving Closures in Effect March 15 through Labor Day
data.description: To protect threatened nesting shorebirds, pets are prohibited in the Otis Pike Wilderness and on federally-managed ocean beaches. Beach driving is closed at Lighthouse Beach, Sailors Haven and the Wilderness area.

data.title: Avian Influenza (HPAI) Suspected in Dead Birds in Seashore Areas
data.description: Dead birds observed along beaches and marsh shorelines may have been infected with Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza (HPAI). The New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (NYSDEC) advises the public to avoid contact with sick or dead birds and mammals that may be infected with HPAI. Pets should be secured at all times and kept away from all wild animals. See the link below for more information.

data.title: March 24 – April 3: Old Stone House will be closed
data.description: The Old Stone House will be closed from March 24 through April 3 for rehabilitation work.

data.title: North Parkway Rehabilitation
data.description: To get more information about the North Parkway rehabilitation, sign up for our newsletter.

data.title: Temporary Closures of Park Entrance Possible if Parking Lots Become Full
data.description: If parking lots become full, there will be a temporary closure of the park entrance at Georgetown Pike & Old Dominion Drive. The park entrance will re-open as soon as space becomes available. For updated information about parking, call Visitor Center at 703-757-3101

data.title: A harmful algal bloom (HAB) has been detected in Government Wash
data.description: Bright green algae (cyanobacteria) floating on the water may contain toxins and is unsafe for swimming or contact. Children and pets are at higher risk if contaminated water is swallowed. Keep pets leashed and away from the water. Seek medical or veterinary care if symptoms occur after exposure. Click More for photos of the harmful algae as well as symptoms in humans or pets if exposed to the bacteria.

data.title: Reds Meadow Road Access Restrictions in 2026
data.description: Reds Meadow Road remains under construction in 2026 and will be closed Mondays through Wednesdays from July 6 through September 20 and Monday through Friday starting September 21. There is no estimate for when the road will open for the season.

data.title: Devils Postpile National Monument facilities are closed for the season
data.description: Devils Postpile National Monument facilities are closed for season, with no public programs, parking, water, or bathroom facilities available.

data.title: Ocean Beach Fire Season in Effect through October 31, 2026
data.description: Fires allowed ONLY in park provided concrete fire rings between Stairwells 15 and 20. Do NOT make fire rings using rocks, logs or other items. For more information, visit the beach fire regulations link below.

data.title: Visitor Center Restrooms
data.description: The visitor center restrooms are currently being remodeled. Restrooms are available in a trailer just behind the restroom remodel.

data.title: 65th Anniversary Event
data.description: Freedom Riders National Monument will commemorate the 65th anniversary of the bus burning and attacks on Freedom Riders in Anniston, Alabama. This milestone event will commemorate those riders that were attacked here in Anniston, providing moments of reflection and education from park rangers and speakers. The event will take place on Saturday, May 9, 2026, the day before Mother’s Day. Go to the event page to learn more. Freedom Riders staff will update this page with updates closer to the event.

data.title: Aquatic Park Bathhouse Elevator Out of Service
data.description: The elevator in the Aquatic Park Bathhouse is currently out of service. Repairs will take roughly 3-4 weeks. The second and third floors may be used for access during operating hours.

data.title: Tisch Mills Segment — Caution: Seasonal Flooding
data.description: The Ice Age Trail from Mill Road to Nuclear Road is often flooded in spring or after heavy rain. To bypass this section, follow CTH-B between CTH-BB and Nuclear Road.

data.title: Pothole Repairs Along the Parkway
data.description: The park is actively working to inspect road surfaces and repair potholes. To report a pothole or initiate a tort claim for personal property damage associated with a pothole, please e-mail GWMP_Superintendent@nps.gov.

data.title: Parking Lot E Closed for Construction
data.description: Parking Lot E along the park's Scenic Drive is closed for construction. The park is currently working to expand this parking area, and the lot will be closed while the expansion is being built. Visitors can still access the Mary Byrd Branch Trail and Quantico Cascades Trail by following trail reroute signs and parking vehicles in Turkey Run parking area. For your safety, please follow all closures and only park in designated parking spots.

data.title: Do Not Handle Volcanic Tephra
data.description: Park staff have cleared Crater Rim Drive East and Chain of Craters Road of tephra which includes volcanic ash, rock and glassy strands called Pele hair. The roads are now open following episode 43 of the Kīlauea eruption. Tephra is still present in many areas at the summit and can irritate your skin, eyes and respiratory system. For your safety, do not touch it.

data.title: Mammoth Hot Springs - Palette Springs Boardwalk Closure
data.description: A portion of the boardwalk leading to Palette Springs, located on the Lower Terraces in Mammoth Hot Springs, will be temporarily closed for repair work.

data.title: Spring Hours
data.description: Visitor Access to Fort Donelson NB Park Lands: Pedestrian Access is from sunrise to sunset. Automobile Access to Fort Donelson NB Park Roads 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily.

data.title: Seasonal Closure: Bush Key
data.description: Visitor access to the north side of Bush Key is suspended through Fall 2026 to protect nesting Sooty Terns, Brown Noddies, and Magnificent Frigatebirds. The south (harbor) side remains open. Boaters must maintain a distance of 100 feet from the island's mean low tide.

data.title: Rehabilitation of Colonial Parkway Continues
data.description: Construction on the Colonial Parkway is affecting travel in many sections. Please follow all detour and caution signs. Additional information can be found at the link below.

data.title: Old Springs Trail near Tennessee Valley closed starting March 23, 2026
data.description: The trail will be closed for boardwalk replacement and general maintenance until early August. Surrounding trails will remain open to provide an alternative route. Please pay attention to safety signage and stay out of the closed area.

data.title: BE AWARE OF HIGH-WATER DANGER
data.description: Visitors should be aware of high-water danger. Heavy rain has made crossing flowing water hazardous for hikers and horseback riders.

data.title: Elevated Fire Risk
data.description: Fire danger is especially high in southeastern Arizona March through June. Red Flag Warnings and fire restrictions may be implemented without notice. Campers: be prepared to go without a campfire and to cook using an enclosed propane device. Be cautious at all times and use water to drown fires. Note: Ground fires and wood collection are prohibited at all times. Stand up grills are available only for overnight campers.

data.title: Flooding and Landslides Affecting Park Complex
data.description: The park complex experienced severe weather events following flood damage in December 2025, causing unsafe conditions and limiting basic infrastructure in some areas. Some services are available in Stehekin with winter and spring operations.

data.title: Large vehicle restrictions come into effect on June 7, 2026
data.description: Beginning June 7, 2026, the park will enforce existing size and weight limits on the Zion-Mt. Carmel Highway. Vehicles that exceed length, width, height, or weight limits will not be allowed to travel between Canyon Junction and the park's East Entrance. See the upcoming limits on the page linked below.

data.title: Bacteria Levels at Arizona Hot Springs
data.description: Bacterial levels in water samples taken from Arizona Hot Springs exceed Local, State and National Park Service standards for recreational activity. Due to the increased human health risk, the National Park Service warns to avoid contact with these waters until further notice. Please contact the Park at 702-293-8990 for updates on this advisory.

data.title: Greenbush Segment — Caution: Logging Activities
data.description: A timber harvest is taking place on the Greenbush Segment. The work affects about 0.1 mile of Trail between Shelter #5 and the Greenbush Picnic Area. Heavy machinery may be in the area. The Ice Age Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

data.title: Annual and lifetime passes not available for purchase
data.description: Annual and lifetime passes are not currently available for purchase at Puʻuhonua o Hōnaunau National Historical Park. As an alternative, you can purchase an annual pass online.

data.title: Quicksand Present at Lakeshore Edge of the Rainbow Bridge Trail
data.description: Quicksand may be present near the shoreline and in drainages throughout the park. It can appear dry and firm on the surface but may suddenly give way. Recognize unstable, shifting, or unusually soft ground, and use caution when entering through these areas.

data.title: Invasive Vegetation Removal
data.description: Watch for crews treating invasive plants along Snake Bight Trail, Rowdy Bend Road, and other Flamingo areas. Approved herbicides are being used. Stay alert for workers, equipment, and posted signs.

data.title: High Levels of Visitation
data.description: The park will be experiencing high levels of visitation from March through May. Expect limited parking, long lines, and crowded areas. Please have a credit or debit card ready or valid park pass with ID when checking in at the visitor center.

data.title: High Levels of Visitation
data.description: The park will be experiencing high levels of visitation from March through May. Expect limited parking, long lines, and crowded areas. Please have a credit or debit card ready or valid park pass with ID when checking in at the visitor center. Parking or stopping is not permitted on the roads outside the park gate at any time.

data.title: DC Water Sewer Overflow
data.description: DC Water is managing a sanitary sewer overflow from the Potomac Interceptor near Lock 10. Anyone with questions or in need of support can reach out to DC Water’s 24-Hour Command Center at (202) 612-3400 or visit https://www.dcwater.com/about-dc-water/media/potomac-interceptor-collapse.

data.title: K-177 Highway Closed from Council Grove South to the Preserve, Use Detour
data.description: Beginning Monday, March 2, K-177 will close for a bridge replacement from Council Grove to the U.S. 50/K-177 Strong City intersection. Tallgrass Prairie National Preserve will remain open but must be accessed from the south or follow the detour west out of Council Grove. K-177 thru traffic will be directed to follow the state detour on U.S. 56, U.S. 56/U.S. 77 and K-150. KDOT uses state highways for official detour routes for safety, weight and maintenance concerns. Go to KDOT website for detour map.

data.title: No dock access to Rainbow Bridge
data.description: At current lake levels, Rainbow Bridge docks do not reach the land. Dock-to-trail access to Rainbow Bridge will not be available until water levels rise again. Restrooms at the dock will remain open. This alert will stay up until the situation changes. Check the link for current lake information.

data.title: Critical Backcountry Updates/Closures
data.description: Visit this link for the inner canyon weather forecast, current trail closures, today's heat risk, and drinking water availability.

data.title: Visitor Center Restrooms Closed for Renovation
data.description: The restrooms at the Kris Eggle Visitor Center will be closed from the end of February until April for renovation. Temporary restrooms will be available in the visitor center parking lot. Visitors in need of more permanent facilities are advised to use those at the Twin Peaks Campground.

data.title: Road Open To: Mile 13 (Mountain Vista)
data.description: The Denali Park Road is open to Mile 13, Mountain Vista. Wintry conditions beyond that point prevent vehicle travel, though pedestrian travel is permitted. Be prepared for winter driving conditions to reach Mile 13.

data.title: Visitor Center Hours of Operation
data.description: The Visitor Center is open Friday - Sunday from 9:00am to 4:00pm

data.title: Several Trails Close For Peregrine Falcon Nesting Season
data.description: Effective March 1, areas including Jordan Cliffs Trail, Penobscot East Trail, Precipice Trail, and Valley Cove Trail are closed to protect nesting peregrine falcons. The parking lot for the Precipice Trail is also closed. Rangers strictly enforce closures.

data.title: Shark Valley Entrance Delays
data.description: Expect long wait times to enter the Shark Valley area particularly between 10am-3pm. The Shark Valley parking lot can fill up quickly and becomes 'one car out, one car in'. We recommend arriving 1.5-2 hours early for any scheduled tram tours. Please call the visitor center at 305-221-8776 with any questions about access

data.title: Lehman Caves Closure
data.description: Lehman Caves and the Lehman Caves Visitor Center are closed to the public as work begins on a six-month project to replace the cave’s 48-year-old, failing electric lights. Project completion is planned for summer 2026. The rest of Great Basin National Park remains open.

data.title: Park Auditorium Closed Until Further Notice
data.description: Due to some damage from recent storms, the auditorium will be closed until further notice. We are sorry for the inconvenience.

data.title: The Painted Desert Oasis Gift Shop, Gas Station and Restaurant are Open!
data.description: The Painted Desert Oasis gift shop, gas station and restaurant located at the north entrance of the park are open, and restrooms are available during construction. Visitor center operations are currently located at the Painted Desert Inn, two miles past the entrance station.

data.title: Fredericksburg Battlefield Visitor Center Closed for Renovations
data.description: Starting November 20, 2025 the Fredericksburg Battlefield Visitor Center will be closed while updates are made to building systems. Front Desk operations including visitor orientation will move to the adjacent Fredericksburg Battlefield Bookstore. The Chancellorsville Visitor Center remains open to visitors 9am - 5pm all week.

data.title: Restrooms and Water Fountains Unavailable at Powers Island and Akers Mill
data.description: Restrooms and water fountains are closed at the Powers Island unit until repairs are complete. Powers Island visitors may use facilities across the river at Cochran Shoals - Interstate North. Restrooms and water fountain at Sandy Point (Akers Mill, West Palisades) are closed for the winter.

data.title: All Trails on Shiloh Battlefield Closed
data.description: Due to tree and limb hazards from the recent ice storm, all trails on the battlefield are considered closed. Cleanup operations have begun on the park. Please be aware of workers and equipment on the roadways.

data.title: March 2: Fishing, ORV, Boat Ramp, Archery Passes on Sale at Recreation.gov
data.description: On March 2, 2026, at 10am, the following passes will be available for purchase ONLINE ONLY through Recreation.gov: Fishing Access (Parkwide), Off-Road Vehicle (Breezy Point), Boat Ramp Parking (Great Kills Park) and Archery Parking (Floyd Bennett Field). Sale Period March 2-March 31. All vehicle decals MUST be picked up by April 12.

data.title: Parking impacted due to the Legacy Restoration Fund Project
data.description: The Lower Farm parking lot near the Mule Barn is closed due to the Legacy Restoration Fund Project. The Upper parking lot located near the Orangery has reduced capacity due to a partial closure because of the Legacy Restoration Fund Project. To learn more about parking at Hampton NHS, click the link or visit the alerts page and then scroll down to the area that talks about parking.

data.title: Glenmont Mansion and Grounds Closed
data.description: Glenmont, the Edison family home and surrounding estate grounds, are currently closed until further notice due to operational and safety considerations. The Laboratory Complex remains open Thursday–Sunday, 10 a.m.–4 p.m. Please check this page for updates.

data.title: Park Headquarters Phones are Down
data.description: Phones at park headquarters are currently down. Please call the visitor center at Painted Desert Inn 928-235-2467 or the Visitor Services office 928-235-2391 between 8 am and 5 pm. You can also send us an email at pefo_information@nps.gov.

data.title: Illinois Memorial Closed for Restoration
data.description: The memorial itself closed for restoration starting on September 16, 2025. Starting on March 2, 2026, access to Tour Stops 2 and 3 will be temporarily closed. The closure point will be at the Shirley House. Visitors will still be able to continue north along Union Avenue and access the remainder of the Tour Road. This section will remain closed for the duration of the Illinois Memorial Restoration. The project is expected to be completed by September 2026.

data.title: Verona Segment — Badger Prairie Park Construction
data.description: Construction at Badger Prairie Park may mean fencing and a small Trail detour west of the parking area. Restrooms and shelters may be closed. The Dispersed Camping Area is available but may have no restroom or water availability. The Trail is open. Stay alert and follow posted detour signs.

data.title: Indian Creek Segment — Caution: Machinery and Parking Closure
data.description: Recent logging near CTH-E (15th Street) may mean heavy machinery and log piles in the area. The parking lot on CTH-E remains closed to hikers. The Ice Age Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

data.title: Monticello Segment — Stewart Tunnel Closed
data.description: The Stewart Tunnel is closed indefinitely pending reconstruction. The Trail is closed at both intersections of Tunnel Road. Use Tunnel Road to detour around the closure. Check with the DNR for details: dnr.wisconsin.gov/topic/parks/badger/tunnel.

data.title: St. Croix Falls Segment — Construction & Intermittent Closures
data.description: Construction activity near the St. Croix Falls school complex may mean machinery in the area. The Ice Age Trail remains open. Trail closures and detours are anticipated in the spring. To bypass when hiking eastbound, head north on Jackson Street, east on Louisiana Street, and south on Blanding Woods Road.

data.title: Point Beach Segment — Partial Trail Closure March 4 to June 15
data.description: The Ice Age Trail and parking area on the west side of CTH-O in Point Beach State Forest will be closed March 4 through June 15 during a timber harvest for public safety. All trails and parking areas west of CTH-O will be closed. To bypass when hiking eastbound, take CTH-O north and continue west around the bend where the road becomes CTH-V. The 2.1-mile reroute replaces 2.0 miles of Trail.

data.title: No Horse-Riding or Pack Animal Permits Issued Until Further Notice
data.description: Horse-riding and pack animal permits will not be issued until further notice. Horses and pack animals should not be brought into the park until permits resume.

data.title: Condition of Turkey Creek Trail
data.description: The South Turkey Creek Trail, located near the Harbor Bay Campground Area, is currently open for hiking for the first mile; however, the remainder of the trail is underwater and cannot be safely accessed.

data.title: Heavy Traffic and High Visitation Alert
data.description: Pinnacles experiences extremely high visitation on weekends, holidays, and during favorable weather, especially in the spring. Expect long lines and delays between 10:00 am and 3:00 pm. If you're planning to hike the High Peaks, Condor Gulch, Balconies, or Moses Spring Trail, it’s recommended to arrive before 8:00 am (parking may fill earlier). Additionally, campground access maybe temporarily delayed during peak periods of visitation when ALL incoming traffic is held.

data.title: Temporary Closure of Right Lane of Clara Barton Parkway
data.description: Temporary Closure of the Clara Barton Parkway Right Lane at Site 17, wooded areas between C&O Canal and Lock 10 parking lot, north to Lock 14, required to address ongoing safety concerns from DC Water emergency sanitary sewer overflow & bypass and pipe repair operations. Click on "More" below for specifica details on the closure.

data.title: 2-hour delayed opening 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather.
data.description: Sunset Crater Volcano National Monument will have a 2-hour delayed opening on 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather and area icy road conditions. The monument will open at 11am.

data.title: 2-hour delayed opening 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather.
data.description: Walnut Canyon National Monument will have a 2-hour delayed opening on 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather and area icy road conditions . The monument will open at 11am.

data.title: Road workers on Park Road in Davis Bayou
data.description: Phase II of the Davis Bayou Bicycle and Pedestrian Path is in process. Visitors may encounter minor traffic delays and flaggers on Eagle Point and Park Road.

data.title: Congress Hall Temporary Closure
data.description: Congress Hall will close to visitors on Monday, February 23 through Friday, April 24, 2026. Contractors will replace fire alarm and security systems to ensure that our visitors, structures and artifacts are protected by up-to-date equipment. These improvements are one more way that the National Park Service is preparing Independence National Historical Park for the upcoming 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence.

data.title: Trail closures possible with winter weather
data.description: Portions of trails may close when snow or ice are present. Please check in at the visitor center for current trail conditions.

data.title: Ice caves are CLOSED due to unstable ice conditions.
data.description: The ice shelf near Meyers Beach at Apostle Islands National Lakeshore was broken up during a strong winter storm that impacted the region beginning on 2/17. Access to the ice caves within the National Lakeshore is closed to visitation.

data.title: Drakes Beach Road open 10 am to 5 pm to better protect elephant seal pups.
data.description: Drakes Beach Road now only open 10 am to 5 pm to better protect elephant seal pups. Northern elephant seals have returned to Drakes Beach and given birth close to beach access points. To better protect the pups during this important time, the parking lot will only be open when park staff and volunteers are present, every day from 10:00 am to 5:00 pm. The beach itself will remain closed, but seals are viewable from the parking lot and Ken Patrick Visitor Center area during these ho

data.title: Overlook Park Lot Unavailable After Dark
data.description: The park parking lot at 72 McBride Extension is open from dawn to dusk every day. Any vehicles left in the parking lot after dusk (i.e. sunset) are subject to towing at owner's expense.

data.title: Historic Homes Closed for Season
data.description: The historic homes are closed for the winter season and are expected to open May 1. Park grounds are open daily sunrise to sunset. The Visitor Center is open 11:00 am to 4:00 pm Mondays through Fridays. The Visitor Center is closed Saturdays and Sundays. The Visitor Center is closed on wintertime federal holidays, except for Presidents Day.

data.title: Speed Limits are Much Lower Inside Park than Surrounding Highways
data.description: Speed limits for the park include 30 MPH near the park boundary and Great Sand Dunes Sign, and as low as 10 MPH near the Headquarters and Entrance Station. Speed limits are in place to protect park visitors, pedestrians, and wildlife in the park. Please drive carefully and responsibly.

data.title: Park Bookstore Has Gone Cashless
data.description: We are excited to announce that our park store, operated by Western National Parks, is now operating on a cashless basis. For your convenience, they accept all major credit and debit cards, as well as mobile payment options.

data.title: Deer management operations
data.description: From January 2 to April 13, 2026, the park will be conducting deer management operations outside of park operating hours. Per the Harpers Ferry National Historical Park Superintendent’s Compendium, trails close 30 minutes after sunset.

data.title: Buildings located on the farm side are closed
data.description: Buildings located on the farm side of Hampton National Historic Site are closed due to construction related to the ongoing Legacy Restoration Fund Project. Visit the project webpage for more information or click the link to learn more.

data.title: Angels Landing Pilot Permit Program
data.description: Everyone who hikes Angels Landing needs to have a permit. Learn why you need a permit and how to get one. This requirement does not affect entering the park or riding the park shuttle (when it is running).

data.title: RV and trailer precautions: be aware of vehicle restrictions before visit.
data.description: As the busy season ramps up, traffic will be denser and can result in delays and complications particularly for owners of longer vehicles. The Tucson Mountain District (west side) of the park can accommodate vehicles up to 40ft in total length on the loop drive and has RV parking available at the Visitor Center, and the Rincon Mountain District (east side) can accommodate vehicles up to 40ft in length on the loop drive but has no RV parking available at the Visitor Center.

data.title: No Water at Hosmer Campground
data.description: As of September 30, 2025, water at Hosmer Campground in the Summit District is shutoff due to a waterline break. Water is available 24-hours at Headquarters Visitor Center one mile up the road. At this point we don't have an estimate for when repairs will be complete.

data.title: Phone Outage
data.description: Our site is experiencing connectivity issues with our phone system, and we are unable to receive calls. Please call 9-1-1 for emergencies. For all other inquiries, please email biho_visitor_information@nps.gov for a quicker response. Thank you for your patience while we resolve this issue.

data.title: Road to the Trailhead Parking Lot is Closed to Vehicles for the Season
data.description: The road leading to the trailhead parking lot at the Big Hole National Battlefield is closed to vehicles for the season but remains open for use by snowshoers & cross-country skiers. The road to the visitor center remains open to vehicles year-round.

data.title: 12/12/2025 Update - Parkway and Campground closed from MP 181-204.
data.description: The parkway is closed to all traffic from MP 181-204. The section of the parkway, including the Jeff Busby Campground and day use area, has been closed since June 9, 2025, to allow for the removal and replacement of failed pavement and culverts. While the project was initially expected to be completed by the end of summer, unforeseen site conditions and weather delays have extended the timeline. A definitive reopening date is not available at this time. Construction will continue into 2026.

data.title: State Route 148 Closed Through Cedar Breaks National Monument
data.description: State Route 148 through Cedar Breaks National Monument is now closed for its routine winter season. Plan alternate routes and check back for updates. Visit the Utah Department of Transportation website for more route information: www.udottraffic.utah.gov

data.title: Application for Special Use Permit
data.description: Each applicant is required to complete a new "Application For Special Use Permit" prior to any new permit being issued

data.title: Roadwork Along Lakeshore Drive at the Alfred Smith Water Treatment Plant
data.description: Starting February 9th, 2026, roadwork will begin on the east shoulder of Lakeshore Drive between the water treatment plant and the Lake Mead Fish Hatchery for the installation of a pipeline. Flaggers will be present during the project, but only minimal traffic delays are anticipated at times that traffic is restricted to one lane. The project is expected to be completed in a few months.

data.title: Folger Park Closed for Rehabilitation
data.description: Folger Park will remain closed until April 30, 2026, at 11:59 pm. This temporary closure is necessary to provide for visitor safety during a comprehensive rehabilitation project to restore park resources and improve the visitor experience.

data.title: South Shore Quinault Road Washed Out Beyond Park Boundary
data.description: South Shore Road – which is maintained by Jefferson County – is closed due to a major wash-out. For more information, visit the Jefferson County website.

data.title: Temporary Visitor Contact Station at Andrew Johnson NHS.
data.description: The Andrew Johnson NHS Visitor Center is currently closed for renovations. A temporary visitor contact station is located inside the Memorial Building (housing Johnson's original Tailor Shop), which is adjacent to the visitor center. Renovation work is expected to take approximately 5 months.

data.title: Annual Closure to Protect Nesting Peregrine Falcons
data.description: To provide peregrine falcons adequate space for nesting, portions of Maryland Heights will be closed from Feb 15 to July 31. This includes several rock outcroppings near the overlook and certain climbing walls. The Maryland Heights Trail is OPEN.

data.title: Staircase, Adjacent Wilderness Areas Closed due to Bear Gulch Fire Impacts
data.description: For a full list of closed trails and campsites, visit our Fire Conditions and Updates page. For archived fire content, including news releases and maps, visit the Bear Gulch Fire InciWeb page.

data.title: Access to Bluestone NSR through Bluestone State Park is closed
data.description: Due to road damage, the road to access Bluestone National Scenic River through Bluestone State Park is closed. The road will remain completely closed as repairs are made. It is expected to reopen in late April or early May.

data.title: Trail Closure at Scott’s Run Nature Preserve (McLean, Virginia)
data.description: Scott’s Run Nature Preserve in McLean, Virginia will close, beginning February 16th, for an urgent sewer project to renew and rehabilitate the Scott's Run Siphon wastewater facility. All trails and parking lots in the park are closed for approximately three months.

data.title: Vehicle Weight Limit at Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens Entrance
data.description: Due to structural conditions at the driveway entrance, access to the Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens main entrance and parking lot is limited to vehicles with a gross vehicle weight rating (GVWR) of 16,000 pounds or less.

data.title: Replace Bocce Ball Court Retaining Wall
data.description: Project work begins on Monday, February 9 to replace the bocce ball 50-foot retaining wall in order to improve existing infrastructure and enhance visitor safety. Several parking spots on lower Van Ness will be fenced off and the bocce ball court will not be accessible for the duration of the project, slated through August 2026. Please be aware of machinery, work, and signs.

data.title: West and East Border Roads closed for public safety
data.description: South Puerto Blanco Drive remains open. All border roads remain closed (the dirt roads directly along the border and all crossover roads leading from South Puerto Blanco Drive to the border) to all public vehicle and pedestrian traffic for public safety.

data.title: Phone Lines Currently Down
data.description: The Whitman Mission NHS phone lines are currently down. If you need to contact us, please email whmi_interpretation. For field trip scheduling, please email whmi_education@nps.gov

data.title: Construction at Muir Home Site
data.description: Construction is ongoing at the home site and the surrounding areas. Disruption to normal visitation may occur.

data.title: Temporary closure of portions of the Carter Barron parking lot
data.description: The south and southwest sections of the parking lot at the Carter Barron Amphitheater (US Reservation 339) are temporarily closed to allow the safe operation of District of Columbia snow storage activities as a result of Winter Storm Fern. See the Record of Determination for more information.

data.title: Life Jackets Required!
data.description: By Nebraska State law, all children 13 years old and younger must WEAR a USCG approved life jacket to float the Niobrara NSR. Anyone over 13 years old must HAVE a life jacket. If you are renting equipment, your outfitter will provide them.

data.title: Staff Unable to Check Phone Messages at the Visitor Center
data.description: At this time, staff are unable to check or receive phone messages left at the visitor center. All calls to the park are being routed to Tuzigoot National Monument. You can also reach the park at moca_tuzi_info@nps.gov if you need to contact the park.

data.title: Hazardous Icy Conditions
data.description: The visitor center at George Washington Birthplace National Monument is open, but travel through park grounds may be limited for visitor safety due to hazardous icy conditions.

data.title: Select Trails Require Day Use Pass from Woodstock Inn & Resort Nordic Center
data.description: Four miles of trail along the carriage road corridors have been designated as ‘Multi-Use Trails’ in winter – walkers are permitted to use these trails free of charge. All other trails require a day/season pass from the Woodstock Inn. See link for details.

data.title: Plan Ahead for 2026 Construction
data.description: Parkwide construction is planned for 2026 and may affect travel in some areas. Colter Bay is anticipated to be a good option for visitors. See the Road Construction page for the latest updates.

data.title: Areas of Meridian Hill Park closed for repairs
data.description: The lower plaza and areas surrounding the cascading fountain of Meridian Hill Park are closed while the park undergoes repairs. Sidewalks along W and 16th Streets remain open to pedestrians. A pathway between W Street NW, the James Buchanan Memorial, the Dante Statue, and the upper terrace is open but will close at a later date for work on the statues. Much of the upper level of the park will close in spring and summer 2026 to rehabilitate the grass.

data.title: Dunes Drive Safety Corridor
data.description: Dunes Drive is open. A safety corridor is in effect between mile marker 3 and 5.5 along Dunes Drive. Visitors may drive but not stop or exit their vehicle in this area.

data.title: Pavement Project- Playalinda District
data.description: The Playalinda Beach District continues a Federal Highways pavement preservation project that started on Sept. 22nd, 2025. Visitors to the seashore could expect limited access to some parking areas and roadways to include the vistas.

data.title: Hopeton Earthworks Closed for Construction Until May 2026
data.description: Hopeton Earthworks, located at 990 Hopetown Rd. Chillicothe, OH 45601, is closed for construction until May 2026. Parking lot improvements and construction activity will block safe access to cars and foot traffic at the trail heads. Call the park at 740-774-1126 for more information or visit the park's Mound City Group Visitor Center at 16062 State Route 104, Chillicothe, OH 45601.

data.title: Click the 'View all alerts' button below for additional park updates
data.description: Be sure to review the ALERT page before and during your visit for additional, up-to-date information on closures, cautions, and other current park conditions.

data.title: Temporary Phone Number for Visitor Center
data.description: Our main phone number for the visitor center is temporarily disconnected. Please call 307-877-4456 to reach a ranger.

data.title: Voicemail not functioning
data.description: The voicemail for the main park phone line (435-692-1234) is not functioning reliably. People are advised to call during business hours in order to speak with a ranger.

data.title: Voicemail not functioning
data.description: The voicemail for the main park phone line (970-562-4282) is not functioning reliably. People are advised to call during business hours in order to speak with a ranger.

data.title: Ohanapecosh Campground area closed for construction into spring of 2026.
data.description: The entire Ohanapecosh Campground area is CLOSED for the duration of the construction project, including all campsites, visitor center, restrooms, picnic area, & Ohanapecosh river access. The campground will open as soon after the completion of the project as possible.

data.title: Backcountry vehicle route closed for winter season
data.description: Vehicular access to Valles Caldera's backcountry is currently closed for the 2025-2026 winter season. During the off-season, the route will remain open to pedestrians, equestrians, cyclists, skiers, and snowshoers. The route is scheduled to reopen to vehicular traffic on May 15, 2026.

data.title: Temporary Closures While World War II Memorial Updates Lighting, Fountains
data.description: The World War II Memorial is undergoing a modernization of its fountains and lighting to address aging infrastructure, improve reliability, and enhance the appearance of the memorial. Visitors may find temporary closures of parts of the memorial during their visit. For your safety and the safety of the contractors, please respect any area closures you encounter. We apologize for any inconvenience.

data.title: Temporary Closures While World War II Memorial Updates Lighting, Fountains
data.description: The World War II Memorial is undergoing a modernization of its fountains and lighting to address aging infrastructure, improve reliability, and enhance the appearance of the memorial. Visitors may find temporary closures of parts of the memorial during their visit. For your safety and the safety of the contractors, please respect any area closures you encounter. We apologize for any inconvenience.

data.title: Selma Interpretive Center Closed (Alternative Center Available)
data.description: The Selma Interpretive Center will remain closed to the public due to major renovations; project completion is expected by 2028. Rangers, parking, and other services are available at a temporary space, the 𝐒𝐞𝐥𝐦𝐚 𝐖𝐞𝐥𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐂𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫, located at 𝟏𝟒 𝐁𝐫𝐨𝐚𝐝 𝐒𝐭. There are two other unaffected visitor centers offering services: Montgomery Interpretive Center (1521 Harris Way, Montgomery, AL 36106) Lowndes Interpretive Center (7002 US-80, Hayneville, AL 36040)

data.title: We Do Not Mail Junior Ranger Books
data.description: We do not mail Junior Ranger books; please download from site. Completed books may be submitted digitally. Junior Ranger badges may be mailed once per person per calendar year after completion. We do not mail completed books back.

data.title: Smith Point Bridge Following Single Lane Traffic Pattern
data.description: Visitors driving to access the Otis Pike Wilderness and Smith Point County Park should be aware that the Smith Point Bridge is open currently following a single lane traffic pattern. There is a 15 ton vehicle weight limit.

data.title: Dredging at Mansfield Channel; Mile 55-60 of South Beach Closed
data.description: Starting January 18, 2026, access to the beach between Mile Markers 55 and 60 will be restricted as the US Army Corps of Engineers dredge the Mansfield Channel and place the dredged materials on land.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Dredging at Mansfield Channel; Mile 55-60 of South Beach Closed

data.title: Tyler Bend Area Closures
data.description: Starting on January 20, 2026, the Tyler Bend Pavilion, Pavilion Parking Area, and Lower Launch river access will be closed to the public due to a construction project. The area is expected to open no later than March 31, 2026.

data.title: Former Golf Course Property
data.description: The former golf course property in Peninsula, including a section of the Valley Trail, is closed during environmental remediation.

data.title: Kennesaw Mountain Road Closed to Private Vehicles and Cyclists
data.description: Starting January 5th, 2026, the road to the top of Kennesaw Mountain will be closed to private vehicles and cyclists due to construction and painting. The park's Visitor Center is still accessible. This work is in preparation for the upcoming Safety Improvement Project. For updates, continue to check park's website.

data.title: Visitor Center open 8:30am to 4:00pm, Wednesday to Sunday.
data.description: To complete an interior renovation construction project, the Visitor Center has been temporarily moved to the opposite end of the Visitor Center parking lot. The temporary Visitor Center is in a wheelchair accessible trailer. Please also note that the lake webcam is offline until construction is completed. Thank you for your patience.

data.title: A Few Small Roads Closed Due to Roadway Damage and/or Repair Work
data.description: These roads are closed due to roadway damage during past storm events and/or repairs to roadway damage: Middle Fork Rd, Redwood Canyon Rd, South Fork Rd, Crystal Cave Rd

relatedRoadEvents.title: Road Closures Related to Road Damage From and/or Repairs Of Past Storm Events

data.title: Winter Hazards on Trails - Downed Trees and High Water
data.description: While park staff clear trails several times each year, please be prepared for downed trees. Rain and wind continue to bring down dead trees within the Carr megafire burn scar, and this trend will continue for years. Also note high water at creek fords and seasonal streams during this time of year. Enjoy your national recreation area but please be safe out there.

data.title: Visitor Center Alert
data.description: Visitor Center is open Friday through Monday 8:30am-4:00pm. Outdoor amenities (bathrooms, passport stamp station, drinking fountain) and grounds remain OPEN DAILY from sunrise to sunset.

data.title: Hospital wing, dining hall, shower room closed for rehabilitation work
data.description: Until approximately 2028, a portion of the cell house will be closed to visitors for important historic preservation work. We apologize for any inconvenience. Learn more at https://www.nps.gov/alca/alcatraz-historic-preservation-projects.htm

data.title: Bridge Closure on FM 2610
data.description: The bridge over Menard Creek on Farm to Market Road (FM) 2610 is closed for construction. Birdwatchers Trail is open and accessible from the south through Romayor.

data.title: Winter Seasonal Road Closure
data.description: The Scenic Drive past the visitor center is closed to vehicles for the winter season. When free of snow, the Scenic Drive is available for pedestrian use, including walking and biking. After it is snow-covered, skiing, snowshoeing, and walking are allowed. The visitor center is accessible and open 8:00 am-4:30 pm Monday through Saturday and closed seasonally on Sundays and federal holidays.

data.title: Expect Long Waits at the Summit Entrance Station Before Sunset
data.description: Due to a high volume of traffic, cars may be temporarily turned around at the gate when capacity is reached. Purchasing a pass online does not guarantee entry at sunset. To ensure timely access, arrive 3 hours before sunset.

data.title: Construction work at entrance to Visitor Center parking lot
data.description: Construction work at the entrance to the Visitor Center parking lot will cause single-lane road closures from January through March. Please use caution and follow signage or directions from workers.

data.title: David Wills House Closure
data.description: The David Wills House is currently closed. No known reopening date is available. Please visit our David Wills House web page for more information and a 3D tour of the house.

data.title: Limited Parking and Traffic Delays in Summit District
data.description: Beginning Monday, January 12, 2026 Haleakalā Visitor Center (summit district) parking lot will have limited parking due to a water infrastructure project. Expect traffic delays. The project will last several months.

data.title: Equestrian Traffic Temporarily Prohibited - Mile 124.5 to 155
data.description: Horses are prohibited between Little Tonoloway (mile 124.5) and Paw Paw (mile 155) due to recent and ongoing towpath resurfacing. See more for details.

data.title: No Boating on Lake Manly (Badwater Basin)
data.description: Launching or recovering any type of watercraft is prohibited. No flotation devices, including kayaks, paddleboards, or similar equipment, are permitted anywhere in the park.

data.title: Cholla Cactus Garden Trail Closed
data.description: The Cholla Cactus Garden trail is closed until late spring, so that park staff can improve trail conditions.

data.title: Going-to-the-Sun Road Seasonal Closure
data.description: Going-to-the-Sun Road is now closed for the season at Lake McDonald Lodge on the west side of the park. On the east side, the closure is located 1.5 miles west of the St. Mary Visitor Center. Travel on open sections of the road may change at any time due to winter weather conditions. Visitors should check road conditions before their arrival and be prepared for winter weather.

data.title: Limited Access to Apollo District Parking Lots 2 and 3
data.description: Please be advised that parking areas #2 and #3 of Apollo Beach district (New Smyrna Beach) will be closed beginning January 5, 2026. These boardwalks are set for demolition and replacement. These areas will be closed to public access during construction. For your safety, please respect all posted closures and avoid entering closed areas of the seashore. Thank you for your patience and cooperation.

data.title: Pet Policy
data.description: Pets are not allowed inside the visitor center at Aztec Ruins or on the trail through the Aztec West Great House. Pets are welcome in the parking lot, the picnic area, and along the Old Spanish Trail. Service animals are the only exception. Emotional support, therapy, and companion animals are not considered service animals and therefore not permitted in the visitor center or on the trail through Aztec West.

data.title: Museum to Remain Open During Construction
data.description: The Oklahoma City National Memorial Museum, Outdoor Symbolic Memorial, and Book Store will remain open during the construction of Foreward - a new Chapter of the museum. Visitors will enter through a new entrance on the southwest corner of the building. A temporary Visitor Experience Center has been placed near the museum for visitors to purchase tickets and browse Memorial merchandise. For more information on Foreward, please visit http://www.memorialmuseum.com.

data.title: Water is Required
data.description: Due to the trail being steep of 12 percent grade, all visitors wishing to hike the Lower Cliff Dwelling Trail must bring a water bottle with them. No Exceptions.

data.title: The Use of Drones is Prohibited at Big South Fork National River & Recreation Area
data.description: Currently, the use of drones is prohibited at Big South Fork and all National Parks. Detailed information about the policy is available...

data.title: Potentially Deadly Poisoning Linked to the Consumption of Wild Mushrooms
data.description: An outbreak of serious illness associated with eating foraged wild mushrooms has been reported. CDPH is urging individuals to avoid foraging for and eating wild mushrooms while risk of amatoxin poisoning is high.

data.title: Park Headquarters Road closed
data.description: Park Headquarters Road is closed due to rehabilitation work on the Sugarlands water and wastewater systems.

data.title: Limited access to some trails due to washouts from winter storms.
data.description: The following trails have been impacted by winter storms: Carter Falls Trail footbridge over the Nisqually River has been dislodged and there is no safe water crossing; Kautz Creek Trail is impassable one mile from the trailhead due to a washout. There may be other trails with unreported storm impacts around the park.

data.title: Homestead Closure
data.description: The historic Andrew Johnson Homestead is closed. All tours and access to this structure are cancelled until further notice.

data.title: Staple Bend Tunnel Closure
data.description: Due to a rockfall, the interior of the Staple Bend Tunnel is closed. The trail is still open. From the Mineral Point Trailhead, visitors can bypass the tunnel, via a path around the right side of the tunnel structure.

data.title: Ride Share Services - pickup limited
data.description: Taxi and ride-share services are often not available in Pecos. Even if services like Uber and Lyft will bring you here, it can be very difficult to secure a ride out. Please ensure you have a departure plan in place before being dropped off at Pecos.

data.title: Accessibility Lifts at Fort Sumter Inoperable Until Further Notice
data.description: The accessibility lifts at Fort Sumter are currently out of order. The only way to access level 2 and level 3 of the Fort is via a staircase until further notice.

data.title: Temporary Pause on Research and Donation Requests
data.description: Please be advised that due to staffing changes and the ongoing hiring freeze, there is a pause on research requests and donation considerations at all parks in the Manhattan Sites Unit until further notice. Please check this website and social media for updates.

data.title: Sand Board and Sand Sled Rentals Not Available In Park.
data.description: Sand boarding and sand sled rentals are located in local communities surrounding the park. Visitors are encouraged to arrange rentals BEFORE coming to the park.

data.title: Parking Reservations Always Required
data.description: Reservations are required for all personal vehicles and shuttle riders visiting Muir Woods. You can make your reservation by visiting www.gomuirwoods.com. Visit our "Know Before You Go" webpage to learn more important details before your visit.

data.title: No Cell Service at Muir Woods
data.description: There is NO cell service at Muir Woods. We discourage scheduling a rideshare service to leave the park. When hiring a taxi, visitors have the option of negotiating a flat rate or using the meter. Under some circumstances a flat rate may be cheaper.

data.title: Park Wide Caution
data.description: Visitors should exercise caution when hiking areas of the park. Hazards on trails and in forested areas such as hanging tree limbs, debris on the ground, and washed-out sections of trails and bridges are possible.

data.title: Park Wide Caution
data.description: Visitors should exercise caution when hiking areas of the park that have opened since Hurricane Helene. Hazards on trails and in forested areas such as hanging tree limbs, debris on the ground, and washed-out sections of trails and bridges are possible.

data.title: Phone Lines Down
data.description: Our phone lines are being upgraded and are currently down. If you need to contact us please email: NISI_Information@nps.gov Or you may call Cowpens National Battlefield at: 864 461-2828

data.title: We cannot mail out at this time
data.description: We do not mail Junior Ranger books; please download from site. Completed books may be submitted digitally. Junior Ranger badges may be mailed once per person per calendar year after completion. We do not mail completed books back.

data.title: We cannot mail out at this time
data.description: We do not mail Junior Ranger books; please download from site. Completed books may be submitted digitally. Junior Ranger badges may be mailed once per person per calendar year after completion. We do not mail completed books back.

data.title: America the Beautiful Pass not Issued at Wilson's Creek
data.description: Wilson's Creek National Battlefield is a fee free site. No pass or charge is required to visit. Due to being a fee free site, America the Beautiful Passes are no longer issued. To learn about different pass types and where you can obtain them, including new digital passes, please visit the provided link.

data.title: Washington Monument is closed for maintenance one day each month
data.description: Closures for 2025: Dec 11, 25. Closures for 2026: Jan 8, Feb 3, Mar 4, Apr 6, May 7, Jun 2, July 4, July 8, Aug 3, Sep 10, Oct 5, Nov 11,26, Dec 25. The monument is also closed for the holidays on July 4 and Dec 25 each year.

data.title: Winter Weather Information
data.description: Park grounds remain open in snowy or icy weather, however parking areas are not cleared until 1-3 days after a snowfall ends. Trails and parking lot may be icy or uncleared. Use caution if driving to the park and onsite as conditions may be hazardous.

data.title: Eastern Front Tour Road south of Route 36 - Closed
data.description: The Eastern Front Tour Road south of Route 36 is closed to all vehicles due to a sinkhole near Harrison Creek (stop 4). Walkers and bicyclist are allowed to use the road. Use caution in the Harrison Creek area.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Eastern Front Tour Road south of Route 36 - Closed

data.title: Acadian Cultural Center Temporarily Closed
data.description: The Acadian Cultural Center in Lafayette is temporarily closed due to HVAC issues. Join us at the Prairie Acadian Cultural Center in Eunice for programs and events 5 days a week, Tuesday through Saturday.

data.title: Thomas Stone National Historic Site Closed for Winter
data.description: Thomas Stone National Historic Site will reopen in Spring 2026.

data.title: Winter Ice and Snow Conditions
data.description: Please use extra caution when visiting the park during winter. Park staff work hard to ensure that parking areas and sidewalks are treated as quickly as possible, however most of the park grounds and trails are not treated during the winter months. So, be careful out there and remember to dial 911 in the event of an emergency. Park staff will be notified by emergency dispatch.

data.title: Reef Ride Bike Days 2026
data.description: For this special event in honor of National Public Lands Day, the Scenic Drive will be closed to motor vehicles for part of the day on Saturday, September 19 and Saturday, September 26, 2026. The road will be closed to vehicles from sunrise to 3 pm to allow for bicycles and pedestrians to enjoy the road at a more leisurely pace. The main road through the park (UT highway 24) - will remain open and unaffected by this closure.

data.title: Park Visitor Center Is Closed
data.description: Due to damage from the 2024 Hurricanes. The Park's visitor centers and restrooms are closed due to health and safety issues. The Park has installed portable restrooms located at the front of the park for visitor use.

data.title: Visitor Center and Bookstore Open Wed - Sun
data.description: Visitor Center and Bookstore hours are Wed - Sun 10-4. It is .5 mile walk to the historic area from the Hikers Lot. Please keep this in mind. If you have access needs, please call the number posted in the parking lot.

data.title: Cascade River Road Winter Closure
data.description: Cascade River Road is closed to vehicles at milepost 21 (Johnson Cabin) two miles before the trailhead. Foot and bicycle traffic is permitted beyond the gate.

data.title: Winter Hours
data.description: December 1, 2025, through March 7, 2026, (change to Daylight Savings Time) all sites are open from 10am to 4pm EST.

data.title: Most Park Roads Not Maintained for Winter
data.description: Sand Point Road is the only park road maintained in winter. Miners Castle Road, Chapel Road, Beaver Road, and H-58 between Grand Marais and Adam's Trail are not maintained.

data.title: Winter Hours: 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM
data.description: From Labor Day through Memorial Day, the Birthplace will be open from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM. The tour schedule remains the same.

data.title: Fish cleaning station at Flamingo is temporarily closed
data.description: The fish cleaning station at the Flamingo marina is closed from Dec. 1 for a few weeks, while we await a new part for the grinder.

data.title: No Potable Water in Park Campgrounds
data.description: There currently is no water available in any campground in Great Basin National Park.  Water is available at some local businesses.

data.title: SR 165 Carbon River/Fairfax Bridge is closed: no access to Carbon River or Mowich Lake.
data.description: There is NO public access to Carbon River & Mowich Lake from SR 165. The bridge is closed to pedestrians, bicycles and vehicles and there is no alternate route.

data.title: Guided Tours at the Reconstructed Fort
data.description: Regularly scheduled guided tours provide access into furnished rooms and explore the fort’s role in Westward Expansion and the reconstruction of the fort in 1976.

data.title: Hart Mine Road Closed
data.description: Hart Mine Road Closed.

data.title: NPS Visitor Wifi
data.description: Visitor wifi and wifi calling is now available in The Kelso Depot parking lot for calls including emergencies. Go to the bulletin board at the Kelso Depot restrooms for instructions.

data.title: EV Charging Stations Unavailable
data.description: Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging stations are currently unavailable in the parking facility due to an ongoing construction project.

data.title: Capacity Restrictions at the Reconstructed Fort
data.description: The design of the reconstructed fort fails to meet accessibility, fire, and life-safety requirements of 1976. A lack of code compliant components such as emergency lighting, illuminated exit signage, panic hardware on doors, and outwardly swinging exit doors results in a limitation of 49 occupants for visitor operations within the reconstructed fort.

data.title: Plaza Access to Reconstructed Fort
data.description: Entry into the reconstructed fort is limited to the central plaza on scheduled days. For your safety respect all closures and restrictions. Guided tours provide access into additional areas.

data.title: Visitor Services Available Only at the Site
data.description: All visitor Services are available at the Historic Site, 23 miles northeast of Eads, Colorado. The Center for Sand Creek Massacre Studies located in Eads, Colorado is available for appointment only research requests.

data.title: 8 Miles of Unpaved Road to Reach Park
data.description: In order to reach the Sand Creek Massacre Site, visitors must travel on at least 8 miles of unpaved roads. We are not able to guarantee road conditions of county roads 54 or W after spring storms.

data.title: A Short Walk is Required to Reach the Fort
data.description: A 1/4 mile (1,275 feet) walk on a paved trail from the parking area is required to reach the gate of the fort. At the end of your visit, you'll return to your vehicle along the same path.

data.title: Visitor Centers are Closed for the Season
data.description: All park visitor centers have closed for the season. This includes the Houghton, Rock Harbor, & Windigo/Ozaagaateng Visitor Centers.

data.title: Isle Royale National Park to Reopen April 16, 2026
data.description: From November 1 - April 15 annually, Isle Royale and its surrounding islands are closed to all visitors. Lake Superior waters are open to navigation. All islands reopen April 16. Call (906) 482-0984 to discuss trip planning, obtain permits, or pay fees.

data.title: Seasonal Operations
data.description: Governors Island National Monument will be closed this winter. Both Castle Williams and Fort Jay will be closed and access to NPS staff will be extremely limited. The rest of the island will be open for visitors. Check https://www.govisland.com/ for island winter events and ferry schedule. Look to this page for re-opening information in the spring of 2026. Use our contact link for questions and requests. Questions or requests should be directed to our contact link on this website. Thank you!

data.title: Springhill Barn: repairs and maintenance
data.description: The historic Spring Hill Barn is closed until further notice for repairs and maintenance.

data.title: Parking at Boundary Access Areas
data.description: Visitors are reminded to park in designated parking areas when accessing the park via boundary trailheads. Parking in unauthorized locations may result in your vehicle being towed at the owner's expense. Park on the right side of the road approaching Thompson Hollow. Parking is authorized up to the "No Parking" sign before a private driveway.

data.title: Trail closure for pipeline restoration project
data.description: The VC09/South Valle Toledo/Posos Trail from the Valle Toledo parking area to the eastern park boundary is closed for restoration. The NPS is working on a trail reroute and will provide details as soon as possible.

data.title: Backcountry Trail Conditions
data.description: GPS is necessary on backcountry trails: Oakridge, Boggy Gut (formerly River), and Kingsnake. These trails may be difficult to follow.

data.title: Water in campground is shut off for the winter
data.description: Due to overnight freezing temperatures, water in the campground has been shut off for the season. Visitors should plan to bring their own water. Small containers may be filled at the visitor center during business hours.

data.title: Washington Monument closed one day each month for maintenance
data.description: Closures for 2025: Dec 11, 25. Closures for 2026: Jan 8, Feb 3, Mar 4, Apr 6, May 7, Jun 2, July 8, Aug 3, Sep 10, Oct 5, Nov 11,26, Dec 25. The monument is also closed for the Holidays on July 4 and Dec 25 each year.

data.title: Site Closure Beginning July 8, 2024
data.description: The Edgar Allan Poe National Historic Site remains closed for a utilities improvement project. Check back in 2026 for a reopening date.

data.title: Delta-01 Tours Not Available at this Time
data.description: Minuteman Missile National Historic Site is unable to offer ranger-guided Delta-01 tours at this time. Existing tour reservations will be honored. An alternative virtual tour of the Delta-01 Launch Control Facility is available online or at the visitor center as staffing allows.

data.title: Volcano Top Access Closed to Vehicles over 26 feet long and to all Trailers
data.description: The Volcano top access is closed to all RVs, buses, attached pull trailers, or any other vehicle over 26 feet in length due to public safety concerns and limited parking.  Trailer drop-off and RV/bus parking are available at the visitor center.

data.title: Fire Restrictions
data.description: Fire restrictions have been lifted at Carlsbad Caverns which means that fires such as charcoal fires in grills and the use of camp stoves or portable grills will be allowed. Igniting and maintaining an open fire must be done with materials that are commonly used for warmth and food preparations such as natural firewood or charcoal briquettes. Other materials such as flammable liquids, garbage, plastic, aerosol canisters, or other artificial substances are not permitted.

data.title: Winter Hours of Operation
data.description: Effective immediately, Martin Van Buren National Historic Site is in winter hours of operation. Both Lindenwald (the historic home of President Martin Van Buren) and the park visitor center are closed to the public.
