Visitor operations, including guided house tours and visitor center services, are anticipated to resume at a TBD date in May/June 2026. The park grounds remain open daily from 7:00 a.m. until dusk. Please note that no visitor services are available during this period.

data.title: Winter Season Closures
data.description: The following regular winter season road closures are in place: Westside Road, Paradise Valley Road, Stevens Canyon Road, White River Road, Sunrise Road, State Routes 410/Chinook Pass (between Crystal Mountain Blvd. & Morse Creek) and 123/Cayuse Pass (at the park boundary).

data.title: Sections of moat wall closed until Summer 2026
data.description: Sections of the moat wall that were damaged by Hurricanes Irma (2017) and Ian (2022), as well as several hurricanes in 2024 are undergoing repair and reinforcement. For your safety, please follow posted signage and do not cross any barricades.

data.title: Chatham Closed for Rehabilitation Project
data.description: Chatham's interior will be closed until at least summer 2026 for a major rehabilitation project. The grounds remain open sunrise to sunset, and the gated parking area will be open from 9-5 daily.

data.title: Beach Access Ramp Closure
data.description: Beginning November 17, the Fort Matanzas beach ramp will be closed to the public for ramp and parking lot improvements as well as the construction of a new public access boardwalk to the beach. The NPS anticipates this construction will take place through May of 2026. During this time no public access by FOOT or VEHICLE is allowed. We appreciate your patience and understanding as we work to improve Fort Matanzas National Monument. This closure does NOT affect Ferry Boat operations to Fort Matanzas.

data.title: All lava tube caves closed November 1 through April 30
data.description: All park lava tube caves are closed for the winter from November 1 through April 30 to protect hibernating bats.

data.title: Closed Tuesdays and Wednesdays
data.description: Winter hours are in effect: the visitor center and park trails are open five days a week, from Thursday to Monday. Closed on Tuesdays and Wednesdays. There is no trail access when the visitor center is closed.

data.title: Park Unable to Receive Phone Messages
data.description: At this time, phone messages cannot be left at the visitor center. Please email the park at moca_tuzi_info@nps.gov or call during operating hours if you need to contact the park.

data.title: Winter Visitor Center Hours
data.description: The Visitor Center is currently open five days a week, Wednesday to Sunday, from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. See Operating Hours and Seasons for more information.

data.title: Visitor Center Closed for the Winter Season
data.description: The visitor center is now closed for the season. Due to limited staffing, we are unable to offer tours till we reopen in the summer. For more information, please call 530-667-8119 or email us at tule_information@nps.gov.

data.title: Park Information Center - Temporarily Moved
data.description: Please Note: The Park Visitor Center at 450 Auburn Avenue is closed for renovations. For Park information visit Park Rangers at Fire Station No. 6 (39 Boulevard N.E. Atlanta, Georgia) Monday - Sunday, 9:00 am to 5:00 pm. Visitor parking GPS: 423 John Wesley Dobbs NE Atlanta Georgia. View the Park calendar for daily Ranger led tours & talks.

data.title: Loop Road and North Entrance Seasonal Closures
data.description: The Loop Road and North Entrance are closed to motorized vehicles until May 2026. These areas are accessible by foot, bike, ski, and snowshoe. Restrooms and campsites are accessible but not maintained in the off-season. Learn more about winter recreation and seasonal closures below.

data.title: Katahdin Overlook Construction Closure
data.description: The Katahdin Overlook is currently closed for construction. Visitors are not permitted in the overlook area due to construction hazards. All other locations on the Loop Road are accessible by motor vehicles seasonally and by foot year round.

data.title: Seasonal Closures
data.description: Jumonville Glen, Mount Washington Tavern and the Picnic Area have closed for the season. They will reopen on May 1, 2026!

data.title: Big Savage Tunnel To Close for Winter
data.description: Somerset County will close the Big Savage Tunnel for the 2025-26 winter season on Wednesday, November 26. This seasonal closure, performed annually, protects the lining of the tunnel from freeze-thaw cycles brought on by high winds, cold temperatures, and wet, snowy conditions at high elevation. Please note that there is no detour, and local roads are narrow and treacherous. Please plan your winter excursions elsewhere on the GAP. The tunnel will reopen in late March or early April, depending on weather.

data.title: Winter Hours Begin on October 1, 2025: 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM
data.description: Little Bighorn Battlefield will be moving to our winter hours beginning October 1st. All available facilities will be open at 8:00 AM. The Tour Road, Last Stand Hill, and Reno-Benteen area will close at 3:30 PM. The contact station, bookstore, and national cemetery will close at 4:00 PM.

data.title: Oversized Vehicles are Unable to Park in Visitor Center Parking Lot
data.description: Due to construction, oversized vehicles will not be allowed to park in the visitor center parking lot due to lack of space and ability to turn-around safely. There is oversized parking along the main park road.

data.title: The Park is ONLY OPEN Friday, Saturday, and Sunday.
data.description: The park is closed Monday through Thursday. Visitors can expect this closure to last through the foreseeable future. There is no access to any part of the park during closures. For questions call 406-924-9167.

data.title: Medano Pass Winter Season Closures in Effect
data.description: The Medano Pass Primitive Road is closed to through traffic for the winter season.

data.title: Weir House, Weir Studio, Young Studio, Visitor Center, and Restrooms Closed
data.description: Please note all park buildings are closed for the season through April. Park grounds are open daily year-round and maps, brochures, and passport stamp are available in the parking lot kiosk. Restrooms are not available during the off-season.

data.title: Visitor Center Open at 419 Decatur Street
data.description: The New Orleans Jazz NHP & Jean Lafitte NHP&P have a combined French Quarter Visitor Center at 419 Decatur Street. Open Tuesdays through Saturdays, 9:30 am to 4:30 pm. Calendar for events: https://www.nps.gov/jazz/planyourvisit/calendar.

data.title: The Picnic Area, Burial Ground, and Potomac River Beach are open Wednesday through Sunday
data.description: The Picnic Area, Washington Family Burial Ground, and Potomac River Beach are open Wednesday through Sunday, 9:00 am to 5:00 pm

data.title: The Visitor Center is open Wednesday - Sunday from 9:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.
data.description: The Visitor Center is open Wednesday - Sunday from 9:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.

data.title: Moores Creek Visitor Center Open Tuesday-Saturday and Park Grounds Open Seven Days Per Week
data.description: The Moores Creek Visitor Center/Bookstore is open Tuesday to Saturday from 9 am until 5 pm. Park grounds are open seven days per week from 9 am until 5 pm.

data.title: New Park Hours
data.description: As of November 13, 2025, the new hours of operation at Puʻukoholā Heiau National Historic Site are 8:30am –4:30pm. The parking lot gate will close at 4:00pm.

data.title: Boquillas Crossing Port of Entry is open Friday-Monday 9:00 am to 4:00 pm
data.description: Until further notice, the Boquillas Crossing Port of Entry will be open Friday through Monday, 9:00 am through 4:00 pm. It will not change to winter hours at this time.

data.title: Some Park Buildings are Temporarily Closed for Maintenance
data.description: Some park buildings are temporarily closed to clean up after the Fire Suppression System replacement. Closed are Officer's Quarters #2 and some of the Dragoon Barracks. A virtual tour link is below.

data.title: All Lava Tube Caves Closed Due to Hazardous Conditions
data.description: All lava tube caves are CLOSED due to snow and ice. Entry to caves is prohibited.

data.title: Temporary Bridge on North Shore Quinault Rd Removed for Winter
data.description: A temporary bridge on the North Shore Quinault Road was removed for the winter on October 23, 2025. The bridge will be reinstalled next spring as conditions allow. The Graves Creek area cannot be accessed via South Shore Road due to a washout beyond the national park boundary.

data.title: Winter storms and trail damage
data.description: With winter windstorms coming and going, tree and trail damage will be fixed as weather and staffing allows. If there is a tree down or trail damage, please report it to the visitor center or to LEWI_information@nps.gov with exact location of the damage.

data.title: Cliff Dwellings Are Closed During the Winter and Early Spring Seasons
data.description: Cliff dwelling tours and access to cliff dwellings are closed for the season. Twenty miles (45-minute drive) from the park entrance is the Mesa Verde Museum and Spruce Tree House Overlook. Views of ancestral sites are seen along the Mesa Top Loop Road. Click the link for information about visiting in winter.

data.title: Credit/Debit Card Only to pay entrance fees
data.description: Capulin Volcano only accepts credit/debit cards for entrance fees. Our cooperating association Park Store accepts cash, credit/debit cards, and checks. There are limited cash only entrance passes available through our cooperating association Park Store.

data.title: Reservations Required for All Campgrounds
data.description: As of January 1, 2025, ALL developed campsites will be available through reservation ONLY. These include Pulltite, Round Spring, Alley Spring, Big Spring, Two Rivers, and Akers. Reservations must be made through recreation.gov (online or by calling).

data.title: Park Hours may Change due to Winter Storms
data.description: Opening delays or temporary closures due to blowing snow, accumulation, and icy roads are likely when these conditions exist. Roads will reopen when snowplows have cleared them for safe two-way travel. Before visiting, check this page for up-to-date information. Colorado Traction Law may apply, see info below. Click link for weather.

data.title: No Water Available in Park
data.description: Water systems throughout the park have been shut off for the winter season, October to April. Please plan ahead and bring your own drinking water.

data.title: Intermittent Lane Closures at GW Parkway for Bridge/Pavement Preservation
data.description: Beginning on Monday, November 17, 2025, expect intermittent traffic impacts at GWMP. This Bridge and pavement preservation work is expected to extend into the Fall of 2026 and will make repairs to 24 GWMP bridges and over 30 paved roads and parking assets. To minimize traffic impacts, weekday lane closures on George Washington Memorial Parkway, Clara Barton Parkway, and Spout Run Parkway will NOT be allowed inbound to DC from 5:30am to 9:30am and outbound from DC from 2:30pm to 7:30pm Monday through Friday.

data.title: Straight Fork and the one-way section of Balsam Mountain Road closed.
data.description: Straight Fork and the one-way section of Balsam Mountain Road (Heintooga Round-Bottom Road) are closed.

data.title: Water Unavailable at Hensley Settlement effective October 22, 2025
data.description: To prevent freezing of and subsequent damage to pipes, all water at Hensley Settlement has been turned off. Hikers should plan accordingly and carry water in sufficient amounts. Any water collected from streams must be purified.

data.title: Upper Fort Mason Roads and Parking Area Closures Fall 2025 - Fall 2026
data.description: Roads and parking areas at upper Fort Mason will have closures during utilities and seismic retrofit projects. Closures planned Fall 2025 through Fall 2026. Lots will be fenced and used as staging areas for projects occurring at Fort Mason. Signs will be posted in advance of utilities work as project progresses through each area at Fort Mason. Read signs and do not park in posted areas. Vehicles will be towed at owner's expense.

data.title: Winter Seasonal Road Closures
data.description: Portions of the Teton Park Road (Taggart Lake TH to Signal Mt.), Moose-Wilson Road (Granite Canyon TH to Death Canyon Road), and other roads are closed to vehicles. Check the Grand Teton Roads page for the latest seasonal and weather related closures. When free of snow, Teton Park and Moose-Wilson Roads are available for pedestrian use, including walking and biking. After they are snow-covered: skiing, snowshoeing, and walking are allowed.

data.title: North Entrance Road and Rim Drive are CLOSED for the Season
data.description: The North Entrance Road and Rim Drive are now CLOSED for the season. They will not reopen until mid to late June or July 2026.

relatedRoadEvents.title: North Entrance Road and Rim Drive are CLOSED for the Season

data.title: The campground is closed for the season.
data.description: The campground is closed for the season. The campground will reopen for the season in Mid-May.

data.title: No Gasoline Available in Park
data.description: The gas station at Mazama Village has closed for the season. No gasoline is available for purchase in the park. Running out of gas will require calling a tow truck to assist. Please plan accordingly!

data.title: Carter G. Woodson Home NHS temporarily closed.
data.description: The Carter G. Woodson Home National Historic Site is temporarily closed due to a full renovation project. We hope to reopen to the public in 2026. Please continue to visit the website for future updates.

data.title: National Cemetery Closed to Vehicles; Open to Pedestrians
data.description: Due to weather related incidents, Vicksburg National Cemetery is closed to vehicular traffic, however it is open to pedestrians. Visitor can park in the adjacent USS Cairo parking lot and walk into the cemetery.

data.title: Louisiana Circle Closed
data.description: Louisiana Circle is closed to vehicles and pedestrians due to slope instability. Navy Circle is open and also provides views of the Mississippi River.

data.title: Seasonal Closure: Gap Cave & Skylight Cave
data.description: Gap and Skylight Caves are closed for the season; access and tours are unavailable. Please check official channels for updates on reopening.

data.title: Beach safety closure in place from Buxton Village to near Ramp 43
data.description: The entire beachfront from the north end of the village of Buxton to near ORV ramp 43 is closed to public entry due to damages to dozens of houses and associated septic systems caused by coastal storm impacts.

data.title: What's Closed and What's Open on South Rim
data.description: Visit this link for a list of the available visitor services in Grand Canyon Village and at Desert View.

data.title: Be City Smart: Take precautions to secure your vehicle and valuables.
data.description: This is an urban park. Don't hide valuables inside your car. Carry items with you on hike. Lock your vehicle and set car alarm. Report suspicious persons loitering/peeking in cars. Call 928-638-7805

data.title: Get Skyline Drive Status Updates
data.description: Sign up for Shenandoah Alerts by texting SHENALERTS to 888777. Any time there are closures, re-openings, or other important safety-related alerts, subscribers will receive a notification via text. You may unsubscribe at any time.

data.title: Big South Fork Announces Temporary Closure of Blue Heron Tipple Bridge
data.description: Big South Fork NRRA has announced the temporary closure of the historic Blue Heron Tipple Bridge, effective immediately in the interest of visitor safety. A recent bridge safety inspection conducted by the Federal Highway Administration identified structural issues specific to the pier’s bridge decking which is now leaning beyond acceptable standards.

data.title: Access to Big South Fork’s Scenic O&W Road Blocked Due to Bridge Failure
data.description: Road access to O&W Road was blocked by order of the State of Tennessee Department of Transportation. Pine Creek Bridge was closed by the state because it was deemed unsafe. The closed bridge is the first bridge on O&W Road after the Toomey Road intersection. The closed bridge is outside of the boundary of the Big South Fork National River & Recreation Area.

data.title: George Rogers Clark NHP in the Eastern Time Zone
data.description: George Rogers Clark National Historical Park is located in the Eastern Time Zone. The memorial closes 15 minutes before the visitor center.

data.title: Water Availability
data.description: There is NO water at the scorpion campgrounds and the trailhead for the cliff dwellings. Water is available at the Visitor Center, Woody's corral and TJ's corral.

data.title: Visitor Pass Needed to Visit George Washington Carver Museum
data.description: All visitors to Tuskegee University, including those visiting the George Washington Carver Museum located on campus, are required to obtain a visitor pass before entering. This policy is part of the university's safety and security procedures. Visitor passes are issued by Tuskegee University Public Safety. Stop by the Campus Public Safety building upon arrival to receive your pass at 1103 West Montgomery Road, Tuskegee, AL 36088.

data.title: New Policy on Fires and Lighting
data.description: Please be advised that a new policy is now in effect regarding the lighting and maintaining of fires within the park. To read the full details, including specific regulations and guidelines, please refer to the Superintendent's Compendium.

data.title: Limited Restroom Availability
data.description: Restrooms are available at the Independence Visitor Center at 6th & Market Streets, the corner of 5th and Chestnut Streets, and the Benjamin Franklin Museum (museum entrance requires a fee) in Franklin Court.

data.title: New Policy on Fires and Lighting
data.description: Please be advised that a new policy is now in effect regarding the lighting and maintaining of fires within the park. To read the full details, including specific regulations and guidelines, please refer to the Superintendent's Compendium.

data.title: Reduced cold-water flow at the Serpentine Hot Springs bathhouse
data.description: Cold water brought in via a pipe helps reduce the temperature of the water in the tub. With the reduced flow, temperatures in the tub are dangerously hot.

data.title: El Calderon Trail closed for construction August 11, 2025 through May 2026
data.description: The El Calderon Trail and parking area are closed from August 11, 2025, through late May 2026 for a major improvement project. NPS Road 100 will remain open for local traffic only, and the Continental Divide Trail will remain open for CDT thru-hikers.

data.title: Lighthouse Area Closed until June 30, 2026
data.description: The area around the Fort Washington Lighthouse is closed until June 30, 2026, at 11:59 p.m. as construction continues to rehabilitate the historic lighthouse.

data.title: Intermittent Visitor Center Phone Issues
data.description: The phone system at the Adams National Historical Park Visitor Center is currently experiencing intermittent audio issues.

data.title: Phone Outage
data.description: We are currently experiencing a disruption in our phone service at Lake Clark National Park and Preserve. Until further notice, please contact us via email for all inquiries and assistance. Email Contact: LACL_Information@nps.gov

data.title: Looking for a Grand Canyon view? A warning about mobile devices and map/navigation apps
data.description: Map apps may direct you to Parashant if you search for directions to "Grand Canyon" due the canyon's name in both the national park and monument names. The national park has paved roads and services. Parashant has no services and rugged/remote conditions.

data.title: Delays up to 1 hour are possible at Kelbaker Road Railroad Crossing near Kelso Depot
data.description: In the event there are train siding operations at the crossing near Kelso Depot, delays may be up to one hour. Please plan accordingly for this possibility with extra fuel and drinking water. Union Pacific Contact: 1 800 848-8715

relatedRoadEvents.title: Kelso Railroad Crossing Delays 1 hour or more

data.title: Kelso Depot Visitor Center closed
data.description: Kelso Depot Visitor Center is closed for rehabilitation of the heating and cooling system. We expect to reopen the depot in 2026. The restrooms beside the depot are open.

data.title: Scale House Closed
data.description: The Scale House and Park Store are closed due to damage sustained during recent flooding.

data.title: Use caution on Cima Road
data.description: Use caution on Cima Road due to shoulder washouts.

data.title: Rough and damaged roads
data.description: Drive slowly and exercise due caution when driving in Mojave National Preserve. Rough and damaged roads exist on both paved and unpaved roads. Watch for potholes.

data.title: Lighting and Maintaining Fires Information
data.description: Fires are permitted only in designated areas and under specific conditions. A fire may be ignited and maintained only by using fuel sources designed and commonly used for warmth or the preparation of food, such as charcoal briquettes or natural firewood. Lighting or maintaining a fire with other materials including, but not limited to, flammable liquids, garbage, fireworks, plastics, aerosol canisters, batteries, or other manufactured or synthetic materials, is prohibited.

data.title: South Unit Address for Mobile Maps – Theodore Roosevelt NP
data.description: On all maps used on mobile devices, use the following address to reach the South Unit Visitor Center and Entrance Station 201 East River Road N. Medora, ND 58645

data.title: Petrified Forest Trailhead Access Update: Use Approved Route
data.description: Directions to the Petrified Forest trailhead have changed. Please use the approved legal route from I-94, Exit 10 toward Camels Hump Lake and follow the clearly marked posts. This update helps maintain good cooperation with neighbors and respect park boundaries. For precise directions, use the link provided.

data.title: Dingmans Falls Trail, Road, and Access Closed for Bridge Replacement
data.description: Dingmans Falls site is closed for a bridge replacement project and trail rehabilitation. All traffic, including pedestrian, cyclist, and vehicle traffic, is prohibited on the road and trail during construction.

data.title: Trails Not Maintained During Winter
data.description: Trails at River Raisin National Battlefield Park are not maintained during winter months. Please use caution.

data.title: Trail Closure at Veterans Park at Balls Bluff (Leesburg, Virginia)
data.description: Veterans Park in Leesburg, Virginia, is closed for park improvements. All trails in the park are closed until Spring 2026.

data.title: Piñon Flats Campground, sites can be reserved 3 months in advance for 2026
data.description: Due to the need for improvements in Piñon Flats Campground, sites can be reserved 3 months in advance beginning in 2026.

data.title: Yellow Jacket (Vespula) Wasp Activity
data.description: Be aware of aggressive ground-nesting yellowjacket wasps. When threatened, leave the area. Do not swat wasps. If stung and you begin to experience serious symptoms or allergic reactions, call 911.

data.title: Attention Visitors, Cab drivers, Uber drivers, and transportation service drivers
data.description: DO NOT drop off your passengers at the visitor center. No trail access. Visitors must be driven to a trail system from the visitor center. If passengers want to go to the visitor center, WAIT FOR THEM in the parking lot until they come back out.

data.title: Road Construction Continues on a Section of Brotherton Road in Chickamauga Battlefield
data.description: The Brotherton Road closure, between Alexander's Bridge and LaFayette Roads, remains in effect as rehabilitation continues. During the closure, all public traffic is prohibited from using the closed section.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Closure of a Section of Brotherton Road in Chickamauga Battlefield

data.title: Patriots Point Ferries Currently Wheelchair Inaccessible
data.description: Ferries to Fort Sumter from Patriots Point are not currently accessible for individuals who use mobility assistance devices or have trouble navigating stairs. Visitors with these needs should depart from the Fort Sumter Visitor Center at Liberty Square.

data.title: Park Store is Cashless
data.description: The Western National Parks store accepts credit/debit cards and contactless/mobile payments only.

data.title: Fire Restrictions Are In Effect Throughout Zion National Park
data.description: Fire Restrictions are in effect throughout Zion National Park.

data.title: Vehicle Length & Trailer Restrictions
data.description: No large vehicle turnaround after Faraway Ranch Parking (1 mile after entrance). Vehicles over 29 feet park at Faraway. Disconnect trailers at Faraway. Trailers with campground reservations proceed to camp. Scenic drive = 24 ft limit and no trailers.

data.title: No Pets are allowed on the Cliff Dwelling Trail
data.description: Pets are not allowed onto the Gila Cliff Dwelling Trail. Kennels are available at the Trailhead on a First Come First Serve basis and are available to those that are going on the Cliff Dwelling Trail not the Wilderness trails.

data.title: Fort Moultrie Fishing Dock Closure
data.description: The Fort Moultrie Fishing Dock is currently under construction through Spring 2026.

data.title: For Visitor information or assistance, Call (334) 727-1247
data.description: For visitor information or assistance at Tuskegee Airmen National Historic Site, please call (334) 727-1247.

data.title: Park Store To Go Cashless
data.description: Starting August 14th, 2025 the park store will go cashless. All major credit cards, debit cards and mobile electronic payments will be accepted.

data.title: Technical Difficulties with Phones
data.description: Oregon Caves phone system is down. For general park info, please call the Illinois Valley Visitor Center at 541-592-5125. Thank you for your patience and understanding.

data.title: Limited Amphitheater Accessibility
data.description: Construction vehicles are operating through the parking lot & Lower Overlook Park - use caution & be vigilant for hazards. Lower Overlook & Amphitheater access is via stairs only for construction duration.

data.title: North Abutment Overlook Closure
data.description: The North Abutment deck leading to the overlook is in the process of being replaced. There is no visitor access to the North Abutment overlook until construction is complete.

data.title: Campground Closures
data.description: The following campgrounds are closed pending repairs: Pulltite (sites 1-13) and Dee Murray. Day use areas remain open for visitors.

data.title: Partial Reopening: Cross Estate Gardens
data.description: The Cross Estate gardens have reopened as of August 4. However, a portion of the trellis area remains closed due to storm damage and ongoing safety concerns. Please do not enter the roped-off section until repairs are complete.

data.title: WNP Bookstore is Cashless
data.description: The Western National Parks (WNP) bookstore has moved to a cashless operation at Timpanogos Cave National Monument. Purchases can be made with credit and debit cards as well as contactless payment methods including Google Pay, Apple Pay, and Samsung Pay.

data.title: William Johnson House Reopens!
data.description: The first floor exhibit hall of the William Johnson House is now open to the public daily from 8:30 am to 5:00 pm. Due to construction, the second floor family living quarters remain closed at this time.

data.title: Phone Lines Down
data.description: Chamizal National Memorial is experiencing temporary phone outages. We apologize for the inconvenience. Information regarding our hours of operation can be found under Plan Your Visit and questions can be directed to cham_information@nps.gov.

data.title: Tickets are required to enter Timpanogos Cave
data.description: Reservations are strongly recommended. Reservations are the only way to ensure a spot is available for your party. Please plan accordingly by visiting Recreation.gov to secure your tickets in advance. Availability of walk-up tickets is not guaranteed.

data.title: Extended Mt. Auburn Street and Sparks Street Closure
data.description: Local road closures and detours on Mount Auburn Street and Sparks Street, and a lane closure on Memorial Drive, may affect traffic and directions to the site. Please observe posted detour and traffic change signs.

data.title: Steel Creek Ranger Station Closed
data.description: The Steel Creek Ranger Station is closed. Please call Tyler Bend Visitor Center at 870-439-2502 for information about the park's Upper District.

data.title: Portion of the boardwalk is closed
data.description: The last section of boardwalk is closed for ADA accessibility. Sorry for the inconvenience.

data.title: Shenandoah National Park Moves to Fully Cashless Fee Collection July 1
data.description: On July 1, 2025, the park transitioned to a fully cashless system at our fee entrance stations. Only credit cards, debit cards, or mobile payments will be accepted.

data.title: Boardwalk Partially Closed for Construction
data.description: The lower section of the boardwalk loop is closed for construction at this time, and the Weston Lake Overlook is now open. Please click "more" below for a map highlighting the closed section and alternate routes.

data.title: No Recreational Vehicle (RV) Parking
data.description: Our parking lot cannot accommodate RVs or trailers. Check out our FAQs page or follow the link for a map to a nearby lot for parking and trailers to detach. RVs should NOT pull into the parking lot.

data.title: Phone lines working intermittently
data.description: Our phone lines are working intermittently. If you are not able to reach us by phone, please email us at bela_information@nps.gov.

data.title: Vehicle Entrance Reservation Not Needed
data.description: Although an entrance pass is required to enter Yellowstone National Park, vehicle reservations are not needed. You are encouraged to purchase an entrance pass before arriving, but you may also purchase passes at any entrance station upon arrival.

data.title: Sherrick Farm Trail Detour
data.description: There is a detour on the trail due to downed trees. Our staff is working diligently to remove these from the trail.

data.title: Charging Stations are Currently Unavailable
data.description: The charging stations at Skyland are currently out of service.

data.title: Use Caution with "Fossil Beds Information" Signs
data.description: The new visitor center is located at 17970 US Hwy 30, about one mile north of downtown Hagerman and the old visitor center. Highway signs are not up-to-date.

data.title: Don't Move a Mussel!
data.description: Hey Boaters, please clean, drain, and dry your vessel completely before setting out on Whiskeytown Lake. Thank you for doing your part to keep the Golden Mussel, an aquatic invasive species, out of the reservoir. Learn more about preventative measures.

data.title: Lakina River Update
data.description: A recently observed landslide across the Lakina River is creating a debris hazard on a popular packrafting route. The river water is cutting its way through, but is highly dynamic and should be expected to remain that way through the boating season.

data.title: Mount Locust Visitor Contact Station and Grounds Open Wednesday-Sunday
data.description: Mount Locust Visitor Contact Station is now open. The contact station and grounds are open Wednesday through Sunday. The historic inn/stand is closed indefinitely due to serious structural concerns. You can still view the inn/stand but cannot enter.

data.title: Belmont-Paul Women's Equality NM exterior lift
data.description: The exterior lift from Second Street to the entrance level is not fully functional. We apologize for the inconvenience. Contact us at bepa_info@nps.gov for more information.

data.title: Cashless Operations
data.description: Black Canyon's operations are now cashless. South Rim Campground is by reservation only via Recreation.gov. East Portal & North Rim campgrounds accept payment for first-come first-served sites ONLY using Scan & Pay through Recreation.gov's mobile app.

data.title: Auto-Theft Warning - Only Bring What You Need to the Park
data.description: You can prevent vehicle break-ins in parking lots and scenic overlooks. Before leaving your car for even a short period of time: 1) Remove all valuables, 2) Lock your doors. 3) Take your cell phones and electronics with you. Read article for more details.

data.title: Water Outage at Ponderosa Campground
data.description: The Ponderosa Campground is experiencing a temporary water outage. Please bring all the water you’ll need for your visit, including drinking water.

data.title: Possible Bridge Closure
data.description: The Circle Trail bridge under Winnewissa Falls may be closed temporarily due to flooding caused by spring rain. Please pay attention to signage posted in the Visitor Center and at the bridge. The rest of the park and Circle Trail remain open.

data.title: Contact Us
data.description: We continue to experience issues with our phone lines, please contact us via our email address: boha_information@nps.gov

data.title: Anchoring Permit Not Required
data.description: Anchoring is allowed without a permit in the designated location on the west side of Buck Island. Updated permitting procedures will be announced at a later date. Check back for updates.

data.title: Stehekin Flash Flood and Debris Flow Area
data.description: The Stehekin area is prone to flooding, which increases in a post-fire environment. Know before you go – visit the Post Fire Safety page for information on what to do in a flash flood and debris flow area

data.title: Major Road Closure in Corinth, MS
data.description: ALERT - Major Road Closure in Corinth, Mississippi. A major bridge replacement has begun on North Polk Street in Corinth, MS, and motorists will need to seek other routes. The work is not estimated to be completed until July or August 2026.

data.title: Possible Delays on Old Mine Road due to Van Campens bridge repairs
data.description: Due to a bridge repair project over the Van Campens Brook, visitors on Old Mine Road may encounter delays over Summer 2025. The road will remain open, with single-lane traffic during working hours.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Van Campens Brook Bridge Repair Project

data.title: Main Street Walpack Bridge Closed Until Further Notice
data.description: Main Street in Walpack Center is closed at the Flatbrook Bridge due to structural damage to the bridge.

data.title: Parking Lot Closure
data.description: Due to ongoing construction, the parking lot is closed. Ample street parking can be found along Dudley Street, including the Brookline Reservoir. For accessible parking, please visit our "Contact Us" page for more information.

data.title: Off Road Vehicle Inspections
data.description: ORV renewals & new permits will be issued at the Nathanial P. Reed Visitor Center Friday-Monday, 9am-1:30pm. No permits will be issued Tuesday-Thursday. Permits will be issued first-come first-served. Visit our ORV page for more information

data.title: Temporary Closure of Select Trails for Rehabilitation Project
data.description: The Old Guides Trail, Heritage Trail, Sunset Point Trail and areas of the Sinkhole Trail are currently closed due to a trail rehabilitation project.

data.title: If You Can't Reach Us By Phone, Please Email Us
data.description: Please call 208-933-4105 to talk to a park ranger. However, if the phone lines are down, contact a ranger through email at hafo_information@nps.gov

data.title: Construction
data.description: There is ongoing construction due to roofing project. Please practice caution on the grounds and do not enter marked areas.

data.title: Kīlauea eruption
data.description: Volcanic activity is episodic, with eruptions separated by pauses lasting several days or more. Eruption can be as short as a few hours. Check webcams and USGS volcano update for current conditions before visiting. Stay safe and follow all regulations.

data.title: Reminder: Look Before Parking
data.description: Visitors are reminded to park in marked parking spaces. Parking in the grass or other alternative areas is unauthorized. Please help us #KeepFortMonroeBeautiful.

data.title: Road Closure and Fishing Pier Impact
data.description: The Engineer Wharf, one of two fishing piers at Fort Monroe is temporarily closed. The Finger Pier remains open. Also closed is the section of Fenwick Road between Ingalls and the East Gate of the fort. Detour signs will aid travelers around the closure.

data.title: Roads May Be Icy - Please Use Caution!
data.description: Even though the sun is shining, the open roads in the park may still have icy patches, especially in the mornings and evenings when the temperatures drop. Please drive with caution.

data.title: Munising Falls Trail Closed Until Further Notice
data.description: The Munising Falls Trail within Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore is closed until further notice. Approximately 75-feet of the paved trail was washed out April 28th from recent heavy rainfall and seasonal snow melt.

data.title: Canyon from Toe of Exit Glacier to the Outwash Plain
data.description: The canyon from the toe of Exit Glacier to where Exit Creek opens into the Outwash Plain is susceptible to sudden outburst flood events. These events produce sudden surges of water, ice and rock. For your safety please stick to designated park trails.

data.title: For Current Visitor Information
data.description: For the most up to date information on Frances Perkins National Monument, please visit the website for the Frances Perkins Center at francesperkinscenter.org.

data.title: Butterfield Trail closed to horses and pack animals.
data.description: The trail remains open for hiking and foot traffic. For more information, see our policy page.

data.title: Pulltite Campground Partial Closure
data.description: Sites 1-13 remain closed. All other sites are open. Sites are available by reservation only - book online at https://www.recreation.gov/camping/campgrounds/234357

data.title: Fort Matanzas Only Accessible by Ferry Service
data.description: Fort Matanzas may only be accessed by our free ferry service. The boats operate Thursday through Monday; we do not operate the ferry on Tuesdays or Wednesdays. For more information about tickets and times, please visit the "Plan Your Visit" tab.

data.title: River Trail periodically closed under I-16
data.description: The section of the River Trail beneath Interstate 16 is closed during bridge construction when pedestrian safety is at risk and remains open when no significant hazards are present.

data.title: Ongoing Construction at the Emmett Till Interpretive Center
data.description: Due to ongoing construction, access to some areas of the Emmett Till Interpretive Center will be limited through May 5, 2025.

data.title: No potable water at Pinnacle
data.description: No potable water is available at the Pinnacle.

data.title: Diablo East Marina Cove, Scuba Cove, and Walkway Access Are Closed
data.description: The Diablo East Marina Cove, Scuba Cove, and walkway access are closed because of the presence of excessive docking material, unsafe walkway and dock, and unstable, loose ground. The area will reopen once these hazards have been corrected.

data.title: Fort Bowie Visitor Center Bookstore is now cashless!
data.description: For your convenience, we accept all major credit and debit cards, as well as mobile payment options. Electronic payment reduces transaction times, save money, and improves accountability. Thank you for your understanding and continued support!

data.title: Talisman-Barrett Beach Dock Closed Due to Ice Damage; Reduced Services for Summer Season
data.description: Due to damage from winter ice, the Talisman-Barrett Beach dock is closed to all public use until further notice. No lifeguards will be present during the summer season. Visitors should pack out trash and expect reduced restroom services.

data.title: North Union Avenue and Grant Circle Closed for Repairs
data.description: North Union Avenue and Grant Circle are closed to vehicular and pedestrian traffic for repairs. Entering these construction areas is prohibited. This work is to repair severe weather damage in 2020. The remainder of the park and tour road are open.

data.title: Ongoing work on Lower Town buildings
data.description: Facilities staff are doing repair work on several buildings in Lower Town. Please follow signs for alternate entrances and heed temporary closures.

data.title: Artifacts Removed From Museum
data.description: Due to leaks and humidity issues, most of the historic artifacts have been taken off exhibit to protect them until these issues have been resolved. Exhibit area is still open including text panels, maps, and hands-on-exhibits.

data.title: Secure trash and food
data.description: Pack it in, pack it out! There are no trash services in or around the monument. All food & trash must be secured inside a vehicle. Ravens routinely open zipped bags & tear into trash bags left outside or in the bed of a truck.

data.title: Park Operating Hours
data.description: Operating hours are 9:00 am to 3:30 pm Wednesday through Sunday until further notice.

data.title: South Unit temporarily closed south of Grand Teton Drive; Trailheads remain open.
data.description: The South Unit of Tule Springs Fossil Beds is temporarily closed to visitor access south of Grand Teton Drive, between Tule Springs Parkway and North Fifth Street in North Las Vegas for visitor safety during construction activities.

data.title: Melrose Mansion Guided Tours
data.description: Reservations for guided tours of the mansion must be made on Recreation.gov. The tour is $11.00 and are at 10, 11, 2, 3, and 4; masks are optional. The estate is open daily from 8:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. The grounds and outbuildings are free.

data.title: Hyde Street Pier Closure
data.description: Hyde Street Pier and access to historic ships will be closed to public until further notice starting 11/04/2024 in preparation for Hyde Street Pier renovation work.

data.title: Preservation Work Along Trails
data.description: Starting March 18, preservationists restoring the bronze state plaques along the entrance road, and the War of 1812 commemorative markers in the Orpheus Grove. Some of these areas may be temporarily closed while work is in progress.

data.title: Glover Archbold Trail detour
data.description: For visitor safety, the southern end of the Glover Archbold Trail is under an emergency closure until further notice due to aerial hazards. Please follow the posted detour along Foxhall Rd. / 44th St. NW / P St. NW.

data.title: For entrance fees, we accept digital passes and credit/debit cards
data.description: Digital passes or credit/debit cards are the ONLY method of payment at all fee collection areas. Pre-paid digital passes are available online and may be purchased in advance.

data.title: Hazardous Conditions on Trails
data.description: Visitors should exercise caution when hiking areas of the park that have opened since Hurricane Helene. Hazards on trails and in forested areas such as hanging tree limbs, debris on the ground, and washed-out sections of trails and bridges are possible.

data.title: Closures Due to Flood Damage
data.description: Some areas of the park including Walnut Canyon Desert Drive and some trails remain closed after extensive flood damage. The main park road to the visitor center and cavern is open.

data.title: Vehicle Break-Ins
data.description: Saguaro National Park is an urban park. Vehicle break-ins have occurred at trailheads around the park. Don't make yourself a target. Leave valuables at home, secure your vehicle, and report any suspicious activity.

data.title: Visitor Center Closed
data.description: The Salt River Bay NHP&EP Visitor Contact station remains closed due to damage caused by Hurricanes Irma/Maria. Reconstruction of the Visitor Contact Station continues.

data.title: Feral Dogs May Be Seen in Organ Pipe Cactus National Monument
data.description: Feral dogs may be present at the Twin Peaks Campground and around the Kris Eggle Visitor Center. Visitors should not attempt to feed or water these dogs or approach them in any way as they may carry disease or become hostile.

data.title: No Hunting Allowed in Castle Mountains National Monument
data.description: Hunting is illegal in Castle Mountains National Monument

data.title: Most Monument Roads Require Heavy Duty 4WD Vehicle to Travel Safely
data.description: Breaking down in the remote monument can be a life-threatening emergency. Most roads require a well-equipped, heavy-duty 4WD vehicle to travel. 2WD high clearance vehicles must use Walking Box Ranch Rd for entry and exit. No Visitor Facilities Available.

data.title: Hozomeen Gate Remains Closed
data.description: Until further notice, the gate at Hozomeen will remain closed; vehicle and foot traffic across the US/Canada border is prohibited. Visitors must enter through a designated port of entry.

data.title: List of trail closures post-Hurricane Helene
data.description: Many closures remain on the Trail following Hurricane Helene's devastation. This will remain true for the foreseeable future. Visit the Appalachian Trail Conservancy (ATC) website for an up-to-date list of closures. OBSERVE ALL CLOSURES. Stay safe!

data.title: Gas Pumps at Panamint Springs Resort are Closed at Night
data.description: Gasoline is available at Panamint Springs Resort from 7am-9:30pm, daily. Gas is available at Furnace Creek and Stovepipe Wells 24hrs.

data.title: Herbert Hoover NHS is Open. The Presidential Library and Museum is Closed for Renovations
data.description: The Herbert Hoover Presidential Library and Museum is closed to museum visitors with plans to reopen in 2026. The Research Room will remain open by appointment, and educational programming will continue during the renovation.

data.title: Reception Center Rehabilitation Project
data.description: The Eisenhower Reception Center is undergoing a rehabilitation construction project in 2025. This will not impact visitors' ability to tour the Eisenhower home. For more, click here.

data.title: America the Beautiful Passes Temporary Unavailable
data.description: At this time all sales of America the Beautiful Passes are temporary unavailable. Please check https://store.usgs.gov/recreational-passes for locations. We will advise when passes are available for sale. We apologize for any inconvenience.

data.title: Apertura retrasada hoy, 10 de enero debido a mal tiempo
data.description: Debido a condiciones del tiempo y riesgos elevados por la mañana, el parque abrirá en retraso a las 9 am el viernes, 10 de enero. Esperamos seguir las horas de atención regulares en el Centro Cultural, pero retrasos o cierres tempranos son posibles.

data.title: Delassus-Kern House and Grounds Closed
data.description: Due to hazardous conditions, the Delassus-Kern House and grounds are closed indefinitely.

data.title: Municipal Pier Permanent Closure
data.description: Municipal Pier is closed permanently. After a safety review with the U.S. Public Health Service, it was determined that the deterioration of Municipal Pier in Aquatic Park Cove is unsafe for public use.

data.title: Important Visitor Information – Screening Process in Effect
data.description: This is a new national park within an active military installation, which includes the current home of the US Army War College. There are no visitor services at this time. Please check back in the spring of 2025 for updates.

data.title: Ongoing Closure Notices
data.description: Devils Island dock and campsites, West Bay Club on Sand Island, and the trail on Stockton Island between Presque Isle Bay and Quarry Bay are closed. These closures affect only a small portion of the Lakeshore. Enjoy your visit.

data.title: Rip Currents and Rough Surf
data.description: Remember that rough surf is dangerous & rip currents can be deadly. Please use caution when swimming at Canaveral National Seashore.

data.title: Holiday Hours
data.description: On Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day the visitor center will be closed (trails will remain open with access to restrooms on the north side of the building).

data.title: Part of Fort Totten Park remains closed for public safety
data.description: Out of an abundance of caution, Fort Totten Park will remain closed east of Fort Totten Drive, south of Gallatin Street/Metropolitan Branch Trail and north of Brookland Ave NE/Farragut St. due to the investigation involving two WWI-era munitions found.

data.title: Part of Fort Totten Park remains closed for public safety
data.description: Out of an abundance of caution, Fort Totten Park will remain closed east of Fort Totten Drive, south of Gallatin Street/Metropolitan Branch Trail and north of Brookland Ave NE/Farragut St. due to the investigation involving two WWI-era munitions found.

data.title: New Mexico Road Advisories
data.description: The New Mexico Department of Transportation updates its map of road advisories regularly. Visitors are encouraged to look for any possible road advisories along their route of travel beforehand.

data.title: Baltimore Area Traffic Advisory
data.description: Traffic in the Baltimore area has increased considerably in recent months due to the collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge which connected the Baltimore beltway. Please check the latest traffic conditions to plan your travel accordingly.

data.title: Trail Conditions
data.description: Rain may cause trails to be slick and overgrown. Staff and Volunteer Trail Crews clear trails regularly. Visitors should use caution and report trail issues to viis_interpretation@nps.gov

data.title: Baltimore Area Traffic Advisory
data.description: Traffic in the Baltimore area has increased considerably in recent months due to the collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge which connected the Baltimore beltway. Please check the latest traffic conditions to plan your travel accordingly.

data.title: Wupatki Pueblo Partial Closure
data.description: 3 rooms at Wupatki pueblo are closed due to an unstable wall. The trail is fully open.

data.title: Temporary Number Change
data.description: The park's phone number has temporarily changed. Please call 434-694-8904.

data.title: Help Prevent Aquatic Invasive Species (AIS) Infestation
data.description: A number of lakes are infested with an array of aquatic invasive species that damage natural ecosystems and waterways. Boaters are advised to Clean, Drain, and Dry all boats and equipment every time they leave the water.

data.title: Visitor Phone Line Intermittent
data.description: You may experience problems reaching park staff via the main visitor phone line which functions intermittently. You may email the park at lavo_information@nps.gov with general park questions.

data.title: Maurice Stephens Parking Lot Closed During Construction Project
data.description: The parking lot near the Maurice Stephens house, located on Rt 23 opposite from the Washington Memorial Chapel, is closed during construction and renovation work on the house. Visitors are encouraged to park in the Chapel lot for Grand Parade trail access

data.title: Migrant Notice
data.description: Like elsewhere in the Florida Keys, we have seen an increase in Cuban migrants arriving by boat from Cuba and landing on the islands of Dry Tortugas National Park. You may observe migrant landings at the park and visitor areas may be impacted.

data.title: Expect Crowding
data.description: Parking lots and popular destinations are crowded especially on weekends and days with warm weather between 10 am and 3 pm.

data.title: Current East Rim Trail Status
data.description: Click "more" to find out if the East Rim trails are open today. In good conditions the trails are open 6 am-11 pm for bikers and as posted for other trail users. In wet or hazardous conditions, the trail will close to all users to prevent damage.

data.title: Black Bears Frequenting the McAfee Knob Area in Virginia
data.description: Human-habituated bears have been frequently spotted in the McAfee Knob Area. Allowing bears access to human food or garbage causes it to lose its fear of humans. Follow all food storage regulations, use provided food lockers and never feed a bear.

data.title: Boardwalks at Watch Hill Partially Closed
data.description: Due to storm damage, partial closure of boardwalks at Watch Hill Nature Trail are in effect.

data.title: Partial Closure of Rocky Springs Trail
data.description: The Rocky Springs section is closed from the Rocky Springs campground at milepost 55 to the northern terminus at milepost 59 until further notice. Visitors should observe posted closures. The trail is unsafe due to trail erosion and deteriorated bridges.

data.title: Lasting Effects from 2021 Dixie Fire
data.description: Some facilities, infrastructure, and areas remain closed; please adhere to all closure signs. In re-opened areas, hazards like fallen trees, loose/falling rock, undefined trails, and hidden stump holes exist. Click more to learn about burned area safety.

data.title: Natchez Visitor Center Closed
data.description: The Natchez Visitor Center is closed for the renovation of the public lobby area. For those seeking visitor information, we direct you to VisitNatchez at the Depot on Boundary Street. Or their website, VisitNatchez.org. We apologize for any inconvenience.

data.title: Closure: Portion of the Awatixa Trail
data.description: The river bank section of trail below the Sakakawea Village and continuing upriver to the parking access with is currently closed due to ongoing erosion and project work.

data.title: Backcountry Camping Closed
data.description: Backcountry camping at the park is closed. There is no established opening date at this time.

data.title: Use Caution in and around Pedersen Lagoon
data.description: On August 7, 2024, a landslide triggered tsunami washed over portions of Pedersen Lagoon near Aialik Bay (59.8842N, -149.7309W). We recommend caution for those visiting the area in and around Pedersen Lagoon. Click more for additional information.

data.title: Bartlett Park Restrooms Closed
data.description: The restrooms at Bartlett Park are closed until further notice due to damage from a large fallen tree.

data.title: Centennial Trailhead Parking
data.description: Reminder: Overnight parking without a camping permit is prohibited. Permits are not issued specifically for parking. Shuttles are available in the Black Hills for through hikers on the Centennial Trail.

data.title: Interdune Boardwalk Closed
data.description: Due to the active safety corridor, the Interdune Boardwalk is closed. Please visit the park visitor center to learn about other options during your visit.

data.title: New Food Storage Guidelines to Reduce Human - Wildlife Interactions
data.description: In response to wolves accessing human food and garbage in and around Rock Harbor and campgrounds located on the east end of the island, new food storage guidelines to ensure the safety of visitors and wildlife are in effect.

data.title: Rehabilitation of Colonial Parkway continues with additional Parkway closures
data.description: Beginning Tuesday, April 9, 2024, construction will close the Colonial Parkway to all travel in either direction between Yorktown and VA Route 199. Follow all detour signs. See additional closure information in the link below.

data.title: Rehabilitation of Colonial Parkway continues with additional Parkway closures
data.description: Beginning Tuesday, April 9, 2024, construction will close the Colonial Parkway to all travel in either direction between Yorktown and VA Route 199. Follow all detour signs. See additional closure information in the link below.

data.title: Park Passes
data.description: Park passes, including: America the Beautiful, Veteran, Lifetime, etc., are UNAVAILABLE for purchase/pickup within Big Cypress. Shark Valley Visitor Center (Everglades) is the closest location to Big Cypress for purchase.

data.title: Canine distemper is believed to be present in the park.
data.description: Contact with saliva from wild animals can transmit distemper to pets, including from but not limited to water bowls, food bowls, trash etc. Distemper is not a threat to humans but can be deadly to pets. Ensure your pets are vaccinated or keep them home.

data.title: Hovenweep has gone cashless
data.description: The park now accepts only mobile or electronic payments for entrance & camping fees. You can reserve campsites and buy entrance passes on Recreation.gov, or only use debit/credit cards to pay in person.

data.title: Natural Bridges has gone cashless
data.description: The park now accepts only mobile or electronic payments for entrance & camping fees. You can reserve campsites and buy entrance passes on Recreation.gov, or only use debit/credit cards to pay in person.

data.title: Sick Birds and Wildlife, H5N1
data.description: Be cautious around sea lions, and dead or sick birds. If you get “bombed” by a bird, wash with soap and water immediately, monitor for signs of illness, and call your doctor. Report beached, sick or distressed wildlife to Wildlife Mortality Reporting.

data.title: Bear Alert
data.description: Black bears inhabit many of the Apostle Islands. Follow safety guidelines: store food and scented items properly, hike in groups, stay attentive, and make noises if you encounter a bear in a visitor area.

data.title: Private Inholdings: Sand & Rocky Islands
data.description: There are privately occupied residences and docks on the southeast corner of Sand Island, as well as just north of Group Site A on Rocky Island. These areas are closed to the public. Please respect their privacy and do not trespass in these locations.

data.title: Overlook Park Parking Lot - Vehicles Must Be Parked in a Designated Spot
data.description: Parking is extremely limited on weekends & event days. Overflow parking is available at the Paterson Museum, located at 2 Market Street. On weekends only, additional parking is available at the lot across from the Museum.

data.title: Mansion ADA Lift is currently not functional awaiting a replacement
data.description: Mansion ADA Lift is currently not functional awaiting a replacement, physical accessibility to the mansion will be limited to those with the ability to climb 3-4 steps into the first floor. We apologize for any inconvenience.

data.title: Jacob Riis Park Access Restrictions
data.description: Due to extremely hazardous conditions Gateway National Recreation Area has restricted access to sections of Jacob Riis Park. Erosion has created unsafe conditions from Bays 1-5, and portions of the eastern beach are closed.

data.title: Wupatki VC Phone Outages
data.description: The main phone line for the visitor center experiences occasional outages. Please email FLAG_Information@nps.gov if you have trouble getting through on the main number.

data.title: Items Worn or Used in Other Caves Not Allowed
data.description: A disease known as White-Nose Syndrome is killing bats in North America. To prevent it from spreading to our bats, do not wear shoes, clothing, or bring any gear into any cave at Carlsbad Caverns National Park that were in another cave.

data.title: Timed Entry Ticket Required to Enter Carlsbad Cavern
data.description: Reservations are highly recommended and can be purchased by clicking "more" or calling 877-444-6777. Reservations only select the entry time. Upon arrival at the visitor center, an entrance pass must be purchased.

data.title: Hiking in Gold Mine Tract
data.description: Visitors are restricted to the designated trails within the Gold Mine Tract in the Great Falls area. This location is generally defined as that area bordered by MacArthur Boulevard and Berma Road and depicted on the Great Falls Maryland Hiking Map.

data.title: Weekend Guided Tours Availability
data.description: Please visit the Guided Tours of the Taft Family Home page to review the tour times available for visitors on weekend day visits.

data.title: No Visitor Center
data.description: Currently, there is no visitor center for Amache National Historic Site. There is a museum in the town of Granada that is operated by Amache Preservation Society. Learn more at amache.org

data.title: BOARDWALK CLOSED along the River Trail
data.description: Due to structural issues, the boardwalk along the river trail is closed until further notice. Access to the Ocmulgee Heritage Trail and river is not available due to this closure. This closure does not affect access to any of the mound sites.

data.title: Inaccurate Third-Party Apps
data.description: Some third-party hiking apps are providing inaccurate trail and safety information to users. Please use the official NPS app for accurate information.

data.title: River Ferry at Akers (on State Highway K) CLOSED until further notice
data.description: The Akers Ferry on State Highway K will not be in service until further notice.

data.title: South Woolum Road Closed, Alternate Route Available
data.description: In the interest of safety, the South Woolum Road has been closed. The Woolum launch and campground remain open.

data.title: Temporary Closure on Santa Rosa Island
data.description: The beaches and dunes of China Camp and Cluster Point, and area in-between, are temporarily closed to all visitor access for the protection of sensitive resources. Travel is allowed on marked paths.

data.title: Most visitors need a parking tag
data.description: All vehicles parking for longer than 15 minutes require a parking tag. Three tag durations are available for purchase for all vehicle sizes and types: Daily ($5), Weekly ($15), or Annual ($40).

data.title: New Screening Facility at The Battery
data.description: The location of the Statue of Liberty and Ellis Island security screening facility and ferry service has moved to a new temporary location next to The View restaurant in The Battery, NYC.

data.title: Park Store is Cash Only
data.description: Currently our cash and debit card reader in our park store is not working. We are only able to accept cash. We apologize for the inconvenience.

data.title: Camping and Docking at Boca Chita and Elliott Keys
data.description: Docking at Boca Chita and Elliott Keys: $25.00 Friday - Monday and Federal Holidays Camping at Boca Chita and Elliott Keys: $35.00 per night. Payments must be made on site using the recreation.gov phone app.

data.title: Williams Ranch Road Closed
data.description: The Williams Ranch Road is currently impassable and closed to public access due to unsafe conditions.

data.title: Only Left Side of Diablo East and Rough Canyon Boat Ramps Are Open - Rest Are Closed
data.description: Due to low water levels, only the left side of Diablo East and Rough Canyon boat ramps are open. Please use caution with Spur 406 and Spur 454 launches, as the water may be too shallow to launch most boats. To see a list of open ramps, click on "more."

data.title: Ticket Required to Hike Old Rag
data.description: March 1 - November 30 those wishing to hike Old Rag are required to obtain a day-use ticket before arriving.

data.title: Martinez Adobe
data.description: The Martinez Adobe, located on the western edge of John Muir’s historic orchard, is currently closed due to structural damage. It is unknown when the public will be able to enter the structure. The Muir/Strentzel mansion remains open to visitors.

data.title: Texas White House Complex Closure
data.description: On January 2, 2024 the Texas White House Complex closed so the construction phase of the Texas White House rehabilitation can begin. The LBJ Ranch driving tour remains open. Visitors can still see the school, cemetery, birthplace and showbarn.

data.title: Park Closures
data.description: From time to time the missile range that surrounds the park performs military testing that may require the closure of the park or Highway 70. Please plan your visit around upcoming military tests.

data.title: Arizona Off-Highway Vehicle (OHV) Requirements for Residents and Non-residents.
data.description: Off-highway vehicles (OHVs) are required by law to display a valid OHV decal to operate on public and state trust lands in Arizona. This includes “street legal” vehicles that meet these two requirements.

data.title: Florissant Fossil Beds has moved to cashless fee collection
data.description: Entrance fees and pass purchases must be made with a credit/debit card or payment app on a mobile device. Electronic payment reduces transaction times, saves money, and improves accountability.

data.title: Lower Crypt Access Subject to Safe Conditions
data.description: Access to the lower crypt area of the Mausoleum is subject to closure during wet and or low light conditions in the late afternoon. The main floor and the two reliquary rooms remain open during these conditions.

data.title: Birth Home Temporarily Closed For Repairs
data.description: The birth home of Martin Luther King, Jr. is temporarily closed for repairs. We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause. All other buildings at the Martin Luther King, Jr. National Historical Park remain open during regular park operating hours.

data.title: Route 7 from Sawmill, AZ is NOT recommended
data.description: Entering the park from the east at Sawmill, AZ on Route 7 is not recommended. Part of this route is UNPAVED and UNMAINTAINED. Please stay on paved roads, such as Indian Route 64 from Tsaile, AZ and Highway 191/Route 7 from Chinle, AZ.

data.title: Hawk Creek Lane Restriction at Lake Roosevelt National Recreation Area
data.description: Due to undercutting action of Hawk Creek on the access road between the Hawk Creek campground and boat launch, the road will be reduced to one lane until further notice. Use caution while on this road.

data.title: Uber, Lyft, and Other "Taxi" Services
data.description: Bandelier is in a remote location, with cell service in the canyon limited to Verizon 4G only. Services like Uber and Lyft will bring you here, but there may be no way to contact a ride out. Please plan your pickup before being dropped off at Bandelier.

data.title: The Use of GPS is NOT Recommended
data.description: Numerous visitors have been routed to remote trailheads and out-of-the-way roads trying to use GPS to reach the main park area or shuttle stop.

data.title: Marsh-Billings-Rockefeller National Historical Park is Cashless
data.description: Tours may be purchased online (recreation.gov) or in person with a credit or debit card only. Cash transactions are not available at the park.

data.title: CCC Camp Picnic Pavilions Closed
data.description: The pavilions in the CCC Camp Picnic Area are closed for the immediate future for restoration work.

data.title: Planning Ahead for Transportation
data.description: If being dropped off at the park, please ensure you will have a return ride. Taxis and rideshares do not often come out to Congaree, and the bus may be unavailable. Please plan ahead.

data.title: Smith Public Use Cabin Closure
data.description: The Superintendent issued a closure of the Smith/40-Mile Public Use Cabin due to being unsafe for public occupancy as a result of structural damage caused by the 2023 Yukon River break-up and flooding.

data.title: Ride Share Services
data.description: Convoy Point grounds, Jetty Trail, kayak launch, and picnic area open at 7 a.m. and close at 5:30 p.m. If you are using a ride share service for transportation to and from the park, ensure ride share services can be onsite before closing time.

data.title: Pozo Nuevo Road to Bates Well Ranch High Clearance, 4-Wheel Drive Only
data.description: High Clearance, 4-wheel drive is required from where Pozo Nuevo Road begins along South Puerto Blanch Drive to Bates Well Ranch area. The road is washed out in areas. Bates Well Ranch is still accessible from the north on Darby Well Road.

data.title: Be aware of changing beach conditions
data.description: Beach conditions are changing frequently. Check current tide charts and weather forecasts before traveling on the beach.

data.title: Jefferson Memorial elevator
data.description: The elevator from the ground level to the chamber of the Jefferson Memorial may be intermittently out of order. Visitors can take steps, but if you require an elevator for a successful visit, please call this number for current status: 202-426-6841.

data.title: Vehicle Break-in and Theft Warning
data.description: Car break-ins in Cuyahoga Valley have tripled since 2022, resulting in theft of personal items like credit cards. Do not leave valuables in your car. As a last resort, lock items in your trunk before arriving. Thieves target parking lots regularly.

data.title: Spring Creek (Queens) Closed to the Public
data.description: Spring Creek is closed to the public at this time. The NPS is conducting field investigations as part of a Remedial Investigation at the site. Spring Creek is located in Queens, NYC.

data.title: All Tickets are First-Come, First Served; We Cannot Take Reservations
data.description: All tickets to the Truman Home and the Truman Farm Home are first-come, first-served. At this time, we are unable to take any reservations for groups. No tickets are issued at the Truman Home itself...all tickets issued at the visitor center.

data.title: Navigation Warning
data.description: Some navigation systems on devices misdirect visitors driving to Wilson’s Creek National Battlefield. Please note that the entrance to the park is at the visitor center located near the Southeast intersection of Hwy ZZ and FR 182.

data.title: 4-wheel drive recommended for beach driving
data.description: 4-wheel drive is recommended if driving on North and South Beach.

data.title: Hawai'i Wildfires
data.description: NPS.gov information page set up for wildfires in Hawai'i.

data.title: Drive Cautiously - Endangered Birds on Roadway
data.description: Nēnē (Hawaiian geese) and ʻuaʻu (Hawaiian petrel) are nesting in the park and may land on or frequent park roads, parking lots, and road leading up to the park. Drivers are reminded to drive at the posted speed limits and exercise caution.

data.title: Auto-Theft Warning - Only Bring What You Need to the Park
data.description: You can help prevent vehicle break-ins in parking lots. Before leaving your car to catch the ferry: 1) Remove all valuables, 2) Lock your doors. 3) Take your cell phones and electronics with you. Read article for more details.

data.title: Auto-Theft Warning - Only Bring What You Need to the Park
data.description: You can prevent vehicle break-ins in parking lots and scenic overlooks. Before leaving your car for even a short period of time: 1) Remove all valuables, 2) Lock your doors. 3) Take your cell phones and electronics with you. Read article for more details.

data.title: Auto-Theft Warning - Only Bring What You Need to the Park
data.description: You can prevent vehicle break-ins in parking lots and scenic overlooks. Before leaving your car for even a short period of time: 1) Remove all valuables, 2) Lock your doors. 3) Take your cell phones and electronics with you. Read article for more details.

data.title: Auto-Theft Warning - Only Bring What You Need to the Park
data.description: You can prevent vehicle break-ins in parking lots and scenic overlooks. Before leaving your car for even a short period of time: 1) Remove all valuables, 2) Lock your doors. 3) Take your cell phones and electronics with you. Read article for more details.

data.title: Limited Cell Phone Coverage
data.description: Cell phone voice and data coverage is very limited in the park and surrounding area.

data.title: Fire Closures - Trail, Area, and Camp Closures
data.description: For closures due to fire activity visit the Fire Closures page, including trail, area, and camp closures for current and post fire conditions.

data.title: Rideshare services should be used with caution if visiting the Tucson Mountain District
data.description: Rideshare services will drop visitors off at the park, but usually will not pick them back up. To avoid being stranded in the park, coordinate two-way trips before using rideshares to visit the West side. No major trails start at the visitor center.

data.title: Wildlife Viewing Guidelines
data.description: San Juan Island National Historical Park provides habitat for many species of animals. When you visit the park, you are visiting their home. Do not approach any wildlife within 75 ft/23 m (two school bus lengths).

data.title: Partial Trail Closure at Portage Lakefront and Riverwalk
data.description: Closed portion of Portage Lakefront and Riverwalk Trail along Burns Waterway due to unsafe conditions from erosional damage. This trail portion will remain closed while funding is sought for repair work. Pier access remains open on either side of closure.

data.title: See TRSite.org for the latest updates on the tour schedule.
data.description: See TRSite.org for the latest updates on the tour schedule.

data.title: Kalawao County Restrictions
data.description: Access to the park, including Kalawao County, the Kalaupapa Trail, the settlement, and Waikolu Valley, is restricted to residents and visitors with approved permits issued by Hawaii State Department of Health. Violations will result in arrest/fine.

data.title: Health and Safety
data.description: Masks are not required but are strongly recommended for all Museum visitors. Children under the age of 5 are not permitted on in-person building tours, but are welcome to attend a neighborhood walking tour or visit us virtually.

data.title: Roadwork may cause delays on US 41/Tamiami Trail
data.description: Current roadwork is ongoing along the park's northern boundary on US Highway 41/Tamiami Trail to raise the road for Everglades Restoration benefits. Drivers may experience up to 15-minute delays and should watch for personnel, equipment and slow downs.

data.title: Mount Tamalpais State Park - Trail Conditions
data.description: Many trails in Muir Woods connect to trails in Mount Tamalpais State Park. For up-to-date information about trail conditions within State Park boundaries, check the Mount Tamalpais State Park Info page (click more) and scroll down to Trail Updates.

data.title: The Steeple Building Closed
data.description: The Steeple Building remains closed until further notice.

data.title: County Line Road Closed Monday To Friday
data.description: County Line Road is closed during the week, from Monday through Friday. The closure will apply to all vehicular, pedestrian, and bicycle traffic. Please find an alternate route.

data.title: Roosevelt Campobello International Park is located in Welshpool.
data.description: Campobello Island is located in the province of New Brunswick, Canada. The physical address for the Visitor Centre is: 459 Route 774 Welshpool, New Brunswick, Canada E5E 1A4

data.title: Passaic River Trail Bridge washed out
data.description: Tropical Storm Ida washed out the Passaic River bridge on the Patriot's Path Trail south of Tempe Wick Road. This area is a known habitat of the endangered wood turtle, so do not wade, walk, or let your dog run in the river at this location.

data.title: Diablo Lake Boat Launch Inaccessible to Powerboats
data.description: Due to silt accumulation, the Diablo Lake boat launch at Colonial Creek South Campground is not usable for launching most powerboats. Expect shallow or no water at the launch ramp and the possibility of needing to carry your boat to or from the water.

data.title: SEASONAL SAFETY MESSAGE
data.description: Be careful and safe while out in the canyon! Be cautious of your surroundings, don't cliff jump, don't stray off of the main trails, and don't get too close to Little River Falls or the canyon rim. These activities could result in injury or death.

data.title: Fort Davis NHS is a NO DRONE Zone!
data.description: The use of drones is prohibited at Fort Davis National Historic Site. Thank you for preserving the cultural landscape.

data.title: Fort Davis NHS is in the Central Time Zone
data.description: Fort Davis National Historic Site is located in the Central Time Zone. The park is open 8:00 am until 5:00 pm (Central Time). The Visitor Center closes at 4:30pm. The Main Gate locks at 5:00pm.

data.title: Toxic Cyanobacteria in the Virgin River and the streams of Zion National Park
data.description: Do not submerge your head in or filter drinking water from still or moving water.

data.title: Dogs Not allowed on ocean beaches in Jamaica Bay and Sandy Hook March 15- Sept. 15
data.description: Dogs are not allowed on ocean beaches at Sandy Hook between March 15 through September 15. Dogs are not allowed on the ocean beaches at Jacob Riis Park, Fort Tilden, Breezy Point Tip and at the bayside of Breezy Point between March 15 through September 15

data.title: Temporary Blackland Prairie Trail Closure to Equestrians
data.description: Heavy rain has undermined a culvert making it unsafe for passage. Due to horse trailer parking availability, the entire section is closed to equestrians. Hikers are still able to travel from the railroad tracks to the Parkway Visitor Center.

data.title: Avoid area around the church & under trees in high wind conditions
data.description: In high wind conditions (sustained winds of 25 mph and above) visitors are advised to avoid the area around the church, with the possibility of falling clay tiles from the roof, and the cemetery, possibility of falling tree branches or limbs. Thank you.

data.title: Special Use Permits
data.description: Special Use Permits are typically needed for activities that benefit a specific group. Examples include: Groups of 40 or more, trips organized by scouting groups, churches, or academic institutions, photography, weddings, and first amendment activities

data.title: Weather Advisory and Storm Warnings
data.description: The park may have delayed openings or closures in the event of severe weather. Travel may not be recommended during these times. Please check local weather forecasts. We appreciate your understanding.

data.title: Weather Advisory and Storm Warnings
data.description: The park may have delayed openings or closures in the event of severe weather. Travel may not be recommended during these times. Please check local weather forecasts. We appreciate your understanding.

data.title: Weather Advisory and Storm Warnings
data.description: The park may have delayed openings or closures in the event of severe weather. Travel may not be recommended during these times. Please check local forecasts. We appreciate your understanding.

data.title: Wind Cave moves to cashless fee collection
data.description: Payments for cave tours, campground fees, as well as Interagency Passes at Wind Cave National Park, will be by credit/ debit cards or digital payments only. Cash and check payments will no longer be accepted.

data.title: For entrance fees, we accept digital passes and credit/debit cards
data.description: Digital passes or credit/debit cards are the ONLY method of payment at all fee collection areas. Pre-paid digital passes are available online and may be purchased in advance.
